programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A cook from a US Navy destroyer tells McGee and Gibbs he believes someone is stalking him with the intention of trying to make him poison the crew, and a picture on a stolen phone leads the team to a group testing the psychological effects of surveillance. Meanwhile, Richard Parsons from the Inspector General's office closes in on Ziva as he investigates the death of Ilan Bodnar, the man who killed her father. Guest starring Colin Hanks (Gus Grimly in the Fargo TV series)

Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when a diplomatic service van is hijacked during the transportation of an unknown package from the State Department. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates when Navy Seal Chad McBride's wife receives a package from Riyadh in Saudi Arabia containing her husband's severed head, which Palmer discovers contains very high levels of radiation. McBride's commanding officer reveals the soldier was on a top-secret reconnaissance mission and asks Vance not to disclose the details to anyone. Meanwhile, Richard Parsons tries to get Gibbs to take responsibility for the many unnecessary deaths for which he holds the NCIS team accountable

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team set up a stake-out at a storage facility to catch a thief suspected of selling hi-tech Navy equipment on the black market, but end up witnessing a murder. To make matters worse, $90million worth of radar gear goes missing while they are distracted. Meanwhile, Ducky asks Abby to run a series of mysterious blood tests, raising suspicions that he is harbouring a secret

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A suspect in a drug-smuggling case is seemingly mauled to death by his own dog, which is immediately scheduled to be destroyed - but Abby refuses to hand it over to Navy officials and launches her own investigation. Meanwhile, Gibbs continues to worry about Shepard's health, convinced she and Ducky are hiding something. Drama, starring Pauley Perrette, Mark Harmon and Lauren Holly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents are investigated by the FBI on suspicion of murdering arms dealer La Grenouille. Jenny Shepard is the prime suspect, as she believed the criminal killed her father some years earlier, so Gibbs forms a plan to help his boss. However, an eyewitness later comes forward and implicates Tony. Starring Lauren Holly and Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Marine is seemingly killed in a mortar attack on America's heavily fortified administrative HQ in Baghdad, but a subsequent autopsy reveals he was murdered. Intelligence analyst Nikki Jardine volunteers to help Tony investigate the case - but her behaviour in Iraq makes him suspect she had an ulterior motive for requesting the assignment

programme.title: NCIS

Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.desc: The team tries to locate a kidnapped girl but has to contend with her grandfather, a former member of a disbanded special forces unit. programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a girl killed in a blaze on board her family's yacht. Meanwhile, Calleigh clashes with her replacement in the ballistics lab

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A teenage girl goes missing while out partying with friends, and a murder investigation is launched when she is found sexually assaulted and strangled in a local park. The team has plenty of leads, with a secret camera hidden in the victim's rented car, and a local playboy admitting to inviting her on board his boat, but the suspects are all keen to point the finger at someone else. David Caruso stars

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Tripp pressures a suspect into confessing to the murder of a divorcee killed on her boat, but Horatio is unconvinced of his guilt and resolves to discover the truth. Subsequent investigations reveal the suspect's fingerprints are on top of the dried blood found at the scene - proving the victim was already dead when he arrived. Starring David Caruso and Rex Linn

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A five-star hotel's pool boy is found dead in its grounds with a key to the penthouse in his pocket. Crime drama, starring David Caruso, Adam Rodriguez and Khandi Alexander

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: As the Doctor investigates reports of strange creatures haunting a London school, he encounters an old friend and her faithful robot companion. Anthony Head guest stars, and Elisabeth Sladen, who accompanied Jon Pertwee and Tom Baker's Doctors in the 1970s, reprises her role as journalist Sarah Jane Smith

programme.title: An Inspector Calls
programme.desc: Adaptation of JB Priestley's stage play. In 1912, a detective (David Thewlis) arrives in a town where a young woman has committed suicide. Interrupting a wealthy family in the midst of a lavish party, he unravels the events that led to the woman's death - an investigation with devastating repercussions as secrets are exposed and each member of the family realises they have all unwittingly played a part. Also starring Miranda Richardson and Ken Stott

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: The annual wine show is a major event in Brokenwood and no one is looking forward to it more than DI Mike, the recent buyer of a 10-acre vineyard. However, when a guest judge and wine critic are found dead, drowned inside a fermenting vat, the show takes second place to the murder investigation. Neill Rea, Fern Sutherland, Pana Hema Taylor and Nic Sampson star

After a visit to the doctor, Gerry decides to quit smoking, and instead of resorting to nicotine patches or gum, he enlists Sandra to help him quit

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: Change is on the way to the sleepy market town of Cranford as the railway pushes out from Manchester, but many of the female residents are more excited about the arrival of the handsome new doctor, Frank Harrison. Adapted by Heidi Thomas, with an all-star cast including Judi Dench and Eileen Atkins, who was awarded a Bafta for her role as the authoritative Miss Deborah Jenkyns

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: The town's residents prepare for Lady Ludlow's garden party, but the occasion is marred by rumours the railway line will come right into Cranford, leaving Deborah feeling betrayed. Dr Harrison's plans to become better acquainted with Sophy are thwarted by Caroline Tomkinson and Clara Smith, Mr Carter takes pity on an illiterate 10-year-old and Miss Matty is shaken to see a face from her past. Starring Judi Dench, Michael Gambon and Eileen Atkins

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: Christmas arrives in the town, but Lady Ludlow regrets any notion of seasonal charity when Mr Carter asks her to intervene in the case of Job Gregson, who has been blamed for a spate of thefts. Miss Matty is delighted when Mr Holbrook invites her to his farm, and Dr Harrison's friend Jack sends Caroline Tomkinson a card hinting she should expect a proposal from the medic. Starring Judi Dench, Michael Gambon and Philip Glenister

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: Miss Matty confides in Mary about Mr Holbrook's death, prompting her to inform Major Gordon of Jessie's regret over not marrying him. Dr Harrison tells Rev Hutton of his intentions toward Sophy and asks permission to court her, blissfully unaware that the generosity he has shown to Mrs Rose has accidentally created an unlikely love triangle. Penultimate episode of the costume drama, starring Judi Dench and Simon Woods

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: The true nature of friendship within Cranford is revealed as the ladies rally to help Miss Matty when she receives terrible news. Meanwhile, Dr Harrison suffers as his career, reputation and relationship all seem ruined, while Miss Galindo and Mr Carter grow closer as they face up to the tragedy of Hanbury. programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Veteran rock band the Flowers of Progress are on the island to record a new album after years of being apart, and with a major sponsorship deal hanging in the balance, tensions are running high. However, their reunion proves to be short-lived when lead singer Stevie Smith is electrocuted in the swimming pool. It seems the victim had a stormy relationship with his brother and fellow band member Jim, while there are rifts between the others and more than one has an axe to grind. But how could any of them have done the deed when they were all in the studio at the time? To add to Humphrey's problems, he is still finding his feet without Camille, although the arrival of new officer JP helps lighten Dwayne's workload. Neil Morrissey and Sally Phillips guest star

programme.title: My Life Is Murder

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Newly promoted DI Reid leads the murder investigation when a busker is stabbed to death down a dark alley in Glasgow city centre. Vital evidence turns up in the hands of two art students who were photographing the area at the time, but as the case progresses, Ross's personal life begins to unravel. Detective drama, starring Blythe Duff, John Michie and Alex Norton, with Siobhan Redmond, Niall Greig Fulton and Steven Pacey

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Nikki investigates a man's apparent suicide and begins to suspect foul play after two more deaths occur, leading to recently released mental patient David Nicholson being accused of their murder. programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Gethin Jones present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Welsh international footballer Hal Robson-Kanu, British athletics great Colin Jackson, rugby league legend Paul Sculthorpe and world champion boxer Terri Harper join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Chef and restaurateur John Torode and food writer and ingredients expert Gregg Wallace judge the efforts of four remaining contestants battling to remain in the contest, tasking them to cook a faultless two-course meal. Lending their thoughts on proceedings are three legends of the series - former champions Phil Vickery, Lisa Faulkner and John Partridge. They know exactly what it takes to get through this competition and are almost certainly in for a treat as the contenders push the boat out in a bid to secure their semi-final place

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Armchair fan Jack turns his comedic sights on Britain's attitude towards its sporting heroes, examining the delight the nation takes in building its sports stars up, as well as bringing them crashing down again. From the red cards and free kicks of David Beckham to Andy Murray's difficult route to the top of tennis and Zola Budd's infamous 1984 Olympic tangle, Jack looks at why it doesn't matter how incredibly talented a person is - if they want to become a British hero, they are not in for an easy ride

programme.title: The Other One
programme.desc: Fed up with Cathy festering in her bed, post-wedding cancellation, and stalking Marcus and Meredith on what was supposed to be Cathy and Marcus' honeymoon, Cat decides the best thing to cheer up her half-sibling is to take her away on their own 'sister honeymoon'. She's got just the place in mind - their late Dad's favourite pub. At the solicitors, Marilyn and Tess learn that Colin's will contains an absolute bombshell and that they must pass on some shocking news to their daughters. Comedy, starring Ellie White, Lauren Socha, Siobhan Finneran and Rebecca Front

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share
programme.desc: Kayleigh has had enough of work and fancies a day off, but John is not having any of it - or is he? Co-written by and starring Peter Kay and Sian Gibson

programme.title: Olympics 2012
programme.desc: A look back at the show produced by Trainspotting and Slumdog Millionaire director Danny Boyle, which marked the start of the London 2012 Olympic Games. The ceremony reflected the key themes and priorities of the event, based on sport, inspiration, youth and urban transformation, and celebrated the contributions made by the UK to the world through innovation, as well as the creativity of British people

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Theo Randall and Shivi Ramoutar, and special guest Bill Bailey joins via video link. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Mary Berry

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond focus on wild food, cooking gnocchi with smoked haddock and wild greens, with chef Billy Boyter and crime writer Val McDermid

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents a look ahead to the weekend's football, including Arsenal v Manchester City and Manchester United v Chelsea in the FA Cup semi-finals

programme.title: Olympics Rewind
programme.desc: The first of four parts of a look back at the Games, including the exploits of cyclists Lizzie Armistead and Chris Hoy

programme.title: Olympics Rewind
programme.desc: Continuing the look back at the Games, focusing on 'Super Saturday', when Jessica Ennis-Hill, Mo Farah and Greg Rutherford all claimed athletics gold medals

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In the suburbs of Bristol, Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich turn a long, pointed front garden into a water-saving, wildlife-friendly space

programme.title: Maleficent (2014)
programme.desc: A fairy falls in love with an ambitious human nobleman, who betrays her in pursuit of claiming the throne. She swears to get revenge on him by placing a curse on his daughter, only to find herself developing a maternal bond with the girl. Fantasy adventure based on the story of Sleeping Beauty, starring Angelina Jolie, Elle Fanning, Sharlto Copley and Imelda Staunton

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys hosts the celebrity version of the classic quiz. Actor Dylan Llewellyn, TV presenter Claire McCollum, digital content creator Caspar Lee, and Radio One DJ Rickie Haywood-Williams return to the spotlight, answering questions on specialist subjects Quentin Tarantino Films, 1980s Television, YouTube and Game of Thrones

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. Those standing behind the podiums this week are actors Michael Sheen, Steve Pemberton and Mark Benton, impressionist Jess Robinson, entertainers Anita Harris and Bobby Crush and former footballers Neil Ruddock and Steve McMahon

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: When Lev is called to help a patient, Evgeny, who has been stabbed, he is delighted to find out that he is Russian, and the pair bond over their similar heritage. However, he soon finds himself reflecting on disturbing and traumatic memories from his own past and his childhood in Russia. Meanwhile, Jan forgets to buy an anniversary gift for her wife, Ffion, and promise to make it up to her, but an emergency means that Jan has to put her work her relationship once more. Elsewhere, Charlie decides to take a step back

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. This time, Mo Gilligan and Anne-Marie pop by for a chat and a game of Musical VAR, and Jamie Redknapp takes on Dina Asher-Smith in The Sporting Fix. Plus, Zara Larsson performs in The Garden Sessions

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents highlights of the Premier League fixture between Norwich City and Burnley at Carrow Road

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Arsenal v Manchester City. Action from the opening semi-final at Wembley Stadium, where the teams battled for a place in the final. The Citizens came out on top in this season's Premier League encounters between the teams, winning 3-0 on both occasions, most recently on 17 June as football returned to action after the lockdown

programme.title: Hot Property
programme.desc: Filly meets his match with eccentric Adam, a louder-than-life student who loves to sing about gonorrhoea

programme.title: Short Term 12 (2013)
programme.desc: A counsellor at a foster care centre for troubled teenagers tries to help a gifted newcomer realise her talents and assists a 17-year-old in dealing with the prospect of having to leave the facility when he turns 18. However, helping them get a good start in life forces her to come to terms with her own difficult past. Drama, starring Brie Larson and John Gallagher Jr

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents highlights of the Premier League fixture between Norwich City and Burnley at Carrow Road

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Arsenal v Manchester City. Action from the opening semi-final at Wembley Stadium, where the teams battled for a place in the final. The Citizens came out on top in this season's Premier League encounters between the teams, winning 3-0 on both occasions, most recently on 17 June as football returned to action after the lockdown

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics England
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from a regional perspective

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Lynne Donnelly wants to move to Australia, so she takes her family for a trial week in Melbourne in the hope of convincing them to leave their home in Scotland for good

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Charles Hanson are in Oswestry, Shropshire, to help two teams look for items, while host Natasha Raskin Sharp has advice on collectibles from occupied Japan

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley finds out about the vicars of Eyam, Derbyshire, who initiated a lockdown 355 years ago to protect the rest of northern England from the bubonic plague

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore returns to transform more items reclaimed from a tip, beginning by rescuing a granite worktop from Altrincham Recycling Centre in Greater Manchester

programme.title: Olympics Rewind
programme.desc: The third part of a look back at the Games, focusing on the second half of the Games - a hectic eight days in which the British gold rush continued in east London

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin goes property hunting with a couple of new grandparents who have £800,000 to buy a home in Wiltshire close to their family. The presenter also takes time out to learn about the traditional process of curing Wiltshire bacon

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Tom Heap are in the Shropshire Hills to find out about an ambitious project to create a nature corridor between the two peaks of Long Mynd and Stiperstones. Tom meets a farmer who's become a convert to environmentally friendly regenerative farming, while Ellie goes in search of rare and much misunderstood wildlife. Charlotte Smith tests the strength of Britain's food supply system in the wake of the pandemic and with Brexit on the horizon

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manchester United v Chelsea (Kick-off 6.00pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the last-four match at Wembley Stadium. The sides last met here in the final of this competition two years ago, when Chelsea prevailed 1-0 courtesy of an Eden Hazard penalty. However, United are unbeaten in the six meetings between the clubs in all competitions and have defeated the Blues three times this season, completing a double over them in the league and winning at Stamford Bridge in the fourth round of the Carabao Cup

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the historic Buckfast Abbey in Devon, where experts examine an 18th-century tankard, an iron chest with a hidden lock dating back to the 1640s, London street signs, a Biba frock, and a piece of shrapnel from the battleship Bismarck

programme.title: The Luminaries
programme.desc: Anna is tried for murder, but Emery is still missing - along with her alibi - and Moody refuses to act on her behalf as he believes the case to be indefensible. However, due to a mix-up the lawyer ends up with Lauderback's trunk and discovers a series of letters from Crosbie that reveal a startling fact. Lydia hosts a seance to make contact with Emery, intending to implicate Anna further, but instead she channels the spirit of Sook and inadvertently accuses Carver of murder. Period drama, starring Eva Green

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Bournemouth v Southampton at the Vitality Stadium and Tottenham Hotspur v Leicester City at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium

programme.title: Looking for Alaska
programme.desc: A truce with the Weekday Warriors proves short-lived, Alaska sets Miles up on his very first date at the school's basketball game, and Takumi has a suspicion who the rat might be

programme.title: Looking for Alaska
programme.desc: Miles agrees to spend Thanksgiving with Alaska, but a trip to the Colonel's home for the holiday that was designed to bring everyone together only leaves the group further apart

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: A metal sculptor, a stained-glass designer and a textile artist pitch ideas for a Derby couple's wedding, with only two being made before the buyers choose their favourite

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: The importance of music in people's lives, through the restoration of a pump organ, an accordion and a bass guitar owned by a member of 1970s disco group Hot Chocolate

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo strikes west across Manitoba into the province of Saskatchewan. High above the Prairie at Riding Mountain, he discovers how a middle-class British boy from Hastings transformed himself into an influential indigenous naturalist called Grey Owl. Deep in the Prairie, he finds a network of railways that once served the wheat farmers of Saskatchewan, and learns how communities grew up around the grain elevators used to load the crops on to rail wagons

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: Built to shelter ancient China from hostile barbarians to the north, the Great Wall also serves as a cultural curtain, beyond which can be found a variety of traditions. This programme goes outside the Wall to meet tribes of reindeer herders on the Siberian border, and explores deserts to the north where ruined cities were once bustling staging posts for camel trains on the legendary Silk Road

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: The Open: Chronicles of a Champion Golfer
programme.desc: A look at how Darren Clarke, Nick Faldo and Rory McIlroy have fared at The Open, featuring contributions from all three golfers

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Hermione Cockburn discovers the secrets to the success of British seaside towns on a visit to Blackpool's Pleasure Beach, and looks back at Rhyl's glory years through Victorian postcards. Neil Oliver also finds out about the people who manned Liverpool's dockyards

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob looks back at the 2012 Olympics opening ceremony, with Danny Boyle and the creative team explaining the challenges that went into creating 'The Greatest Show on Earth'. With exclusive access to behind-the-scenes footage, from auditions through to the night itself, this is the story of 90 minutes that dazzled the world, including interviews with some of the 7,500 volunteers involved in the show, who discuss the friendships that were forged during the show and how it has affected their lives since

programme.title: Britain's Olympic Torch Story
programme.desc: A look back at the 70-day, 8,000-mile nationwide relay that captured the public's imagination in the build-up to the 2012 London Olympics, hearing from some of the 8,000 torchbearers. The film recalls moments including the torch zip-wiring across the Tyne Bridge, crossing the border in Ireland, climbing Mount Snowdon, being part of a marriage proposal and joining in ballroom dancing in Blackpool

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antique experts Catherine Southon and David Fletcher are in Ushaw in County Durham, seeking a selection of objects to take to auction. Catherine falls in love with a model of Muffin the Mule and David is enchanted by a limited edition book by author JK Rowling. Presenter Paul Martin learns what life was like for the students who studied at Ushaw when it was open as a Catholic college

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Part two of two. Michael Portillo continues his 570-mile journey through New South Wales. In the Blue Mountains, he learns about people's relationship to the land from his indigenous guide, before reaching Katoomba, where he rides the steepest railway in the world. He follows this with an overnight journey on one of the world's longest trains, the luxurious Indian Pacific Railway, which takes him to his destination - the mining city of Broken Hill

programme.title: Test Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents action from day two of the Second Test in the three-game series, held at Old Trafford. It is the first time this venue has hosted a Test match since last September, when England lost the Fourth Test of the 2019 Ashes series to Australia. With analysis from Michael Vaughan and commentary by Alastair Cook, Carlos Brathwaite, Phil Tufnell and Alison Mitchell

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Adam Frost brings viewers up-to-date on how his garden has been developing over the summer as well as offering advice on looking after and growing hydrangeas. In Devon, Toby Buckland shares tips on dealing with damaged shrubs, tree pruning and transplanting perennials, while Frances Tophill travels to West Sussex to meet a gardener and professor of biology whose garden has been planted with the conservation of insects in mind. Plus, a second chance to see Carol Klein's in-depth profile of a national collection of hydrangeas at Darley Park in Derby

programme.title: A Wild Year
programme.desc: An exploration of the upland area of North Yorkshire, where heather-clad uplands and sheltered dales have been shaped both by the elements and by its people. Hardy Swaledale sheep roam the high moors all year round only coming down to the lowlands for lambing and shearing. Red grouse feed and breed amongst the heather alongside curlews and other ground nesting birds and billions of purple heather blooms provide nectar for the bees, brought to the moors by beekeepers in the summer. Toby Jones narrates, as time-lapse camera techniques bring fresh insight into the restless rhythms of the region

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Singer Adam Ant presents a retrospective look at the pop culture of 1981, when Kim Wilde, Clare Grogan and Tom Selleck were among the pin-ups. It was the year Britain was introduced to animated crimefighter Danger Mouse, the sport of darts was in its heyday, and the films Gregory's Girl and Porky's hit cinemas

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: After reflecting on the fallout of the first week, Louise discovers something troubling about Tom. Rosamund Pike and Chris O'Dowd star in a comedy, written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Mark Urban presents analysis of the day's national and international events

programme.title: 20th Century Women (2016)
programme.desc: Comedy drama following a teenage boy, his mother and two other women who help raise him among the love and freedom of Southern California in 1979. Starring Annette Bening

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: While the rest of the UK has struggled to contain rising levels of knife and gun crime, Scotland has dramatically reduced violent crime in the past 15 years. But how was it done? Kate Silverton films with Police Scotland's Violence Reduction Unit, which tries to prevent crime by offering more help and compassion to those at risk of offending

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows
programme.desc: When Nandor and Laszlo are abducted by a coven of witches, the others must venture to Brooklyn to rescue them

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows
programme.desc: The vampires prepare to attend the most exclusive event on the vampiric social calendar.

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: India plans an airport-inspired wedding, with a cup cake trolley dash for the passenger wedding guests and some tricky manoeuvring to guide her mother down the aisle

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood introduce another selection of clips, this time featuring pets, including a trip to a rabbit island, and a rat that chases cats

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and friends compete in a pedal car grand prix in Dorset.

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: The team present the last Six Badges of Summer show. Online campaigner Nikki Lilly reveals why viewers should earn their Silver Badge, Richie Driss tells his story in Draw My Life and chart star Becky Hill performs her new single Heaven on My Mind

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall sets out to visit six continents in search of Earth's 60 deadliest animals

programme.title: Wonders of the Moon
programme.desc: Using the latest and most detailed imagery, this documentary reveals the monthly life cycle of the moon as it waxes and wanes. From Wales to Wyoming, and from Hong Kong to Croydon, the programme explores how the moon shapes life across the planet earth, and uncovers more about the moon's journey around our planet and how it delivers one of nature's most awe-inspiring events, a total solar eclipse

programme.title: The Open: Chronicles of a Champion Golfer
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents a look at how Tiger Woods, Tom Watson and Jack Nicklaus have fared at the Open, featuring first-hand accounts from the trio as they recount their victories

programme.title: The Open: Chronicles of a Champion Golfer
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents a look at how Darren Clarke, Nick Faldo and Rory McIlroy have fared at the Open, featuring contributions from all three golfers

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain concludes her culinary road trip by sampling what Wales has to offer. She heads to the Brecon Beacons National Park to meet sheep farmer Richard Roderick, who gives her a crash course in the essential skills of a shepherdess. In return, she cooks him lamb bhuna, served with garlic naan. In Monmouth, Nadiya visits Desdemona Freeman and Denise Jones, the UK's only homegrown edible rosewater makers, harvesting petals and creating a rose and raspberry fool

programme.title: The Good Cook
programme.desc: Food writer Simon Hopkinson demonstrates how to create restaurant-quality food at home, preparing stuffed roasted peppers, steak and chips, aubergines with feta cheese, smoked haddock pilaf, and a rhubarb crumble. He also discusses his love of olive oil and Guernsey cream

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Brian Turner accompanies Diane Louise Jordan on a trip to her childhood home of Hatfield, Hertfordshire, where she remembers her mother's Caribbean cooking and school lunches

programme.title: Royal Recipes
programme.desc: Michael Buerk and Anna Haugh prepare George V's favourite curry, Polly Russell discovers how Francis Drake solved a debt crisis and the secrets of a famous condiment are revealed

programme.title: The Thomas Crown Affair (1968)
programme.desc: A bored millionaire masterminds a bank robbery, only to find himself pursued by a glamorous insurance investigator determined to prove his guilt. It is not long before they get embroiled in a passionate affair - but conflicting interests mean they can never really trust one another. Crime drama, starring Steve McQueen, Faye Dunaway, Paul Burke and Yaphet Kotto

programme.title: Bears About the House
programme.desc: Part one of two. Conservationist Giles Clark embarks on his biggest mission to date - taking on the illegal wildlife trade and helping to build a new bear sanctuary in Laos, south-east Asia. Giles has been asked by good friend Matt Hunt, CEO of Free the Bears, to help for 12 months and is not long into his role when he has to step in to help Mary, a five-month-old sun bear. Mary was rescued after her mother was killed in the wild and was spotted on the back of a truck for sale into the illegal wildlife trade. Fragile and malnourished, she lives with Giles and Matt at home while she needs round-the-clock specialist care

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Part one of two. Michael Portillo rides the transcontinental Indian Pacific Railway from the southern port city of Adelaide to Perth in the west. Along the way he hears the story of how an enterprising 19th-century Briton with a murky past began to grow vines. Michael enjoys the fruits of his labours and hears his story from his great great great granddaughter. Later, the presenter embraces the luxury of a previous age as he travels across Australia's notoriously remote and inhospitable Nullarbor Plain

programme.title: A House Through Time
programme.desc: Historian David Olusoga follows the story of the Liverpool house from 1891 to 1945, a turbulent 50-year period encompassing two world wars and the Great Depression. For most of that time, it was home to the Snewings, a large family of saddlers. David follows their business through challenging times - death in the family, cut-throat competition, the First World War and the age of the motor car. With their departure came Jack Greenall, a docker keeping a secret from his employees, and then the Duffys, a couple both trying to escape unhappy childhoods

programme.title: Golf: The Open
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents a look at Shane Lowry's triumph on home soil at Royal Portrush, setting a new course record on day three en route to winning by six shots in front of a passionate crowd. Actor James Nesbitt narrates

programme.title: Test Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents action from day three of the Second Test in the three-game series, held at Old Trafford. This is the first meeting between the sides here in this format since June 2007, when England prevailed by 60 runs. With analysis from Michael Vaughan and commentary by Alastair Cook, Carlos Brathwaite, Phil Tufnell and Alison Mitchell

programme.title: British History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Historian Lucy Worsley explores how in 1873 the British government enacted a coup to take over the rule of India from the huge and influential Honourable East India Company. The leaders of the company were presented as the villains, while Queen Victoria was elevated to the role of wise and benevolent Empress

programme.title: The Duchess (2008)
programme.desc: Fact-based period drama, chronicling the life of Georgiana Spencer who became Duchess of Devonshire in 1774 at the age of 17. Adored by the public for her extravagant lifestyle, Spencer endured a troubled marriage and was eventually driven into the arms of Charles Grey, a senior Whig politician. Starring Keira Knightley, Ralph Fiennes, Dominic Cooper and Charlotte Rampling

programme.title: Maps to the Stars (2014)
programme.desc: A disfigured woman is hired as an assistant to a fading Hollywood star, who has become obsessed with her dead mother and is determined to recreate her most famous film role. Meanwhile, a teen idol goes off the rails under the influence of his overbearing parents and a chauffeur harbours dreams of becoming a writer. David Cronenberg's drama, starring Julianne Moore, Mia Wasikowska, John Cusack and Robert Pattinson

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: MasterChef's John Torode continues his alphabetical journey, with this episode's recipes linked by the letter S. Mary Berry prepares salmon with a fresh sorrel and spinach sauce, and Nigel Slater cooks sea salt chocolate snaps

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and garden designer Danny Clarke tackle a neglected and boring backyard in Birmingham for its owner Sophie, who wants the area full of birds, bees and butterflies

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani and Matt Baker visit the Blean, an ancient woodland in the heart of Kent where they get an exclusive look at a unique wilding project. There is a report from Gloucestershire on the elusive pine marten, while one of the driest springs on record is causing big problems at Adam Henson's farm

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Adam Frost brings viewers up-to-date on how his garden has been developing over the summer as well as offering advice on looking after and growing hydrangeas. In Devon, Toby Buckland shares tips on dealing with damaged shrubs, tree pruning and transplanting perennials, while Frances Tophill travels to West Sussex to meet a gardener and professor of biology whose garden has been planted with the conservation of insects in mind. Plus, a second chance to see Carol Klein's in-depth profile of a national collection of hydrangeas at Darley Park in Derby

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter demonstrates the difference between a bed of hardy annuals sown in a prepared bed alongside an unprepared bed, and George Anderson shows off his Joppa allotment

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Rick Stein's Spain
programme.desc: The chef continues his culinary tour in Castile - a region famous not only for its vast plains and castles but also as the home of 11th-century military leader El Cid - before heading to La Mancha, an area renowned for its rustic dishes and wine production, as well as being the land of Miguel de Cervantes' literary character Don Quixote

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Dish of the Day
programme.desc: The food writer turns the contents of his weekly shop into seven more meals, showing how items that he often buys can be made more exciting by concocting delicious new flavour combinations. The dishes he ends up with include ricotta beefburger, smoked mackerel coleslaw, and lemon and thyme cake

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: The penultimate leg of Si King and Dave Myers' European baking tour takes them to France. Travelling from Lyon to Carcassonne, they learn the secrets of teeth-pulling nougat, go truffle hunting and meet up with Dave's old flatmate, whom he has not seen for 30 years. Culinary delights en route include tarte aux pralines, a savoury-stuffed brioche, apricot tarte Tatin and, of course, the traditional French baguette

programme.title: Stories of Us
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley and broadcaster Ashley John-Baptiste reveal the inspiring stories of people across the UK who lead spiritually and emotionally fulfilling lives. In this first edition, they set out to meet the people who have found inner peace, calm and equilibrium among the hustle and bustle of modern living. Kate takes to the open waters to experience how wild swimming has been a life-changer for some - and even a life-saver for others. Meanwhile, Ashley discovers how a little time living as a hermit could ease the pressures of busy lives

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: From the RAF Museum in Cosford, Shropshire, where antique experts Jonathan Pratt and Adam Partridge find a First World War pigeon capsule and a large collection of toy cars

programme.title: Olympics Rewind
programme.desc: The fourth and final part of a look back at the Games, focusing on the second half of the Games, and featuring cyclist Chris Hoy claim a second gold medal in the keirin

programme.title: A Wild Year
programme.desc: An exploration of the upland area of North Yorkshire, where heather-clad uplands and sheltered dales have been shaped both by the elements and by its people. Hardy Swaledale sheep roam the high moors all year round only coming down to the lowlands for lambing and shearing. Red grouse feed and breed amongst the heather alongside curlews and other ground nesting birds and billions of purple heather blooms provide nectar for the bees, brought to the moors by beekeepers in the summer. Toby Jones narrates, as time-lapse camera techniques bring fresh insight into the restless rhythms of the region

programme.title: Test Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents action from day four of the Second Test in the three-game series, held at Old Trafford. It is the first time this venue has hosted a Test match since last September, when England lost the Fourth Test of the 2019 Ashes series to Australia. With analysis from Michael Vaughan and commentary by Alastair Cook, Carlos Brathwaite, Phil Tufnell and Alison Mitchell

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Sara Davies, Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman and Tej Lalvani assess the profit-making potential of an eco-friendly entrepreneur's alternative to straws, a range of skincare products, a device designed to make bikes more visible, and a brand of food with a personal story behind it. Evan Davis presents

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan: From My Sofa
programme.desc: Romesh catches up with old friends from Albania, Mongolia and The Arctic, revisiting some of the highlights and introducing never-before-seen moments such as his near-death experience in a high-speed Mongolia car rally

programme.title: Trump in Tweets
programme.desc: Documentary taking an in-depth look at US President Donald Trump's use of social media, examining the impact of his tweets on politics both in America and across the world. With access to people in his inner circle, interviews with experts and journalists, as well as some of his most avid Twitter followers, the programme tells the story of how Trump has used Twitter as a tool to shape policy, conduct diplomacy and wage war on the establishment. Narrated by The Vivienne

programme.title: Donald Trump: All-American Billionaire
programme.desc: In a programme first shown in 2010, well before he became US President, Emily Maitlis profiles the property tycoon, exploring the controversy surrounding his plans to develop a golf resort on sand dunes near Aberdeen. She also investigates how he amassed his multi-billion-dollar fortune, and discovers why his extravagant lifestyle serves to reinforce his business brand. Featuring interviews with Trump himself, as well as members of his family and TV producer Mark Burnett, who helped make the businessman one of America's biggest small-screen stars on the original version of The Apprentice

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A report from Salford as the city lifts the lockdown and tries to get its community back to work. The programme follows the mayor and his team, looking at the burden that has fallen on local councils, and also showing the work done by teachers, bus drivers and those helping the homeless. But for Salford it has been expensive. With the threat of bankruptcy looming, can the council provide the support and services the locals need to get them through the next phase of the health and economic crisis?

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: Advanced paramedic Kirsten is faced with an unusual job. A call has come in from police about a burglar who has injured himself trying to escape from the house he was looting. As she arrives, she finds him in agony with a nasty open fracture on his leg. Later, Kelsey and Dave are called to a pregnant woman in a lot of pain who worries she may be in early labour, and Gareth and Gabby treat a woman who is struggling with her breathing. But then two high-priority calls come in - both of which involve children

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Nicky wakes up in hospital to find her overbearing mother interfering in both her work and personal life. She takes the opportunity to reassess her priorities. Fletch and Essie befriend a cancer patient, but the relationship forces all three of them to confront some ugly truths about what the future holds. Guy tries to prove he still has what it takes, and tries to persuade Max to approve a risky operation

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Nigel Kennedy at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of the musician's performances from the BBC archive, documenting the British violinist and violist's development and career, from a seven-year old child on Town and Around, to his virtuoso show-stopper Czardas from the Last Night of the Proms 2013. Featuring interviews with him through the years, the programme studies his versatility of styles, including classical, experimental and jazz

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Mark Radcliffe provides football fans with a chance to revisit some of the best (and worst) songs produced to celebrate the tournament's conclusion over the years. Ditties such as Manchester United's 1996 song Move Move Move, featuring a youthful Paul Scholes, David Beckham and the Neville brothers, could feature alongside the arguably more 'professional' sounding efforts of the London Community Gospel Choir, which sang Abide With Me - the FA Cup's traditional anthem - ahead of Chelsea's clash with Everton at Wembley in 2009

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents the edition first broadcast on 14 December, featuring performances by Rob'n'Raz, Van Morrison and Cliff Richard, Madonna, Latino Rave, Wet Wet Wet and Tina Turner

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes and Anthea Turner present the edition first broadcast on 21 December, featuring performances by Sonia, Bros, Band Aid II, FPI Project, Duran Duran and the Christians

programme.title: Huey Morgan's Latin Music Adventure
programme.desc: Huey visits New York City, home to over a million Puerto Ricans and the place where he first fell in love with the hot sounds of Latin Music. 'El Barrio' or Spanish Harlem became the focal point for the community, with Latin music the soundtrack to their survival. Fusing traditional music with American R'n'B and soul, newly minted Nuyoricans created the '60s dance craze, the Boogaloo. Flying south to Puerto Rico itself Huey sets off on a journey to discover the music that was left behind, hunting down the roots of Salsa in the capital San Juan, and the traditional folk styles of Bomba and Plena

programme.title: Latin Music: A Session With
programme.desc: A performance by the world renowned Cuatro player, the Latin American guitar made famous in Luis Fonsi's hit song Despacito. The Grammy Award-winning star is joined by family and friends in the mountains of Puerto Rico to present some of his favourite music.

programme.title: Roy Orbison: One of the Lonely Ones
programme.desc: Biography of the singer and musician, casting new light on the triumphs and tragedies that beset his career, featuring performances and home movies, and providing an insight into Orbison's remote Texas childhood and his battles to get his voice heard. This film reveals how his rollercoaster life was often reflected in the dark lyrics of his songs, from success to rejection, and rediscovery in the 1980s with the Travelling Wilburys supergroup. Featuring interviews with Jeff Lynne, T Bone Burnett, Bobby Goldsboro and Marianne Faithfull

programme.title: Southern Rock at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive performances by classic Southern rock acts on shows including The Old Grey Whistle Test, In Concert and Wogan. Featuring Lynyrd Skynyrd, the Charlie Daniels Band, the Ozark Mountain Daredevils, Delaney & Bonnie with Eric Clapton, Dickey Betts, formerly of the Allman Brothers Band, the Marshall Tucker Band, Black Oak Arkansas, Greg Allman with Cher, and Edgar Winter

programme.title: The Defiant Ones
programme.desc: The stories of music entrepreneurs Jimmy Iovine and Dr Dre and their improbable partnership and influence on contemporary culture. Dr Dre was raised in Compton, California, where his fascination with dance music, DJ innovations and sound brought him into contact with Eazy-E, Ice Cube, DJ Yella and MC Ren, who would become the core of the 1980s gangsta-rap group NWA. A native of Brooklyn, Jimmy gravitated to music in a bid to dodge the family business as a longshoreman. Through a mix of hard work and luck, he later connected with artists like John Lennon, Patti Smith and Bruce Springsteen

programme.title: Huey Morgan's Latin Music Adventure
programme.desc: Huey visits New York City, home to over a million Puerto Ricans and the place where he first fell in love with the hot sounds of Latin Music. 'El Barrio' or Spanish Harlem became the focal point for the community, with Latin music the soundtrack to their survival. Fusing traditional music with American R'n'B and soul, newly minted Nuyoricans created the '60s dance craze, the Boogaloo. Flying south to Puerto Rico itself Huey sets off on a journey to discover the music that was left behind, hunting down the roots of Salsa in the capital San Juan, and the traditional folk styles of Bomba and Plena

programme.title: Latin Music: A Session With
programme.desc: A performance by the world renowned Cuatro player, the Latin American guitar made famous in Luis Fonsi's hit song Despacito. The Grammy Award-winning star is joined by family and friends in the mountains of Puerto Rico to present some of his favourite music.

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Lambing season is just a month away for most of the farmers and to the east of Inverness Martin tends to 500 pregnant ewes and gets the birthing sheds ready with help from his dad Stevie. Further west in Argyll, George takes a group of Belgian hunters out on a deer-stalking trip, and later he and wife Sybil co-host a judging competition where knowledge of blackface sheep is put to the test. In the far north, John heads to the vet with a pedigree ewe that's pregnant with triplets

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham finds out about the much-loved dolphin that has started breaking people's bones and reveals how a tiny crustacean has mastered the holy grail of science - the art of invisibility

programme.title: Wonders of the Great Barrier Reef with Iolo Williams
programme.desc: The naturalist travels with a specialist dive team looking for signs of hope that the Great Barrier Reef can survive the threat of global warming. Invited on the journey by award-winning underwater cinematographer and marine biologist Richard Fitzpatrick, Iolo dives deep beneath the tranquil surface of the coral sea to discover what state this natural wonder is in

programme.title: Rockfield: The Studio on the Farm
programme.desc: Documentary telling the unlikely tale of how brothers Kingsley and Charles Ward turned their dairy farm in Monmouth into a world-renowned recording studio, playing host to Black Sabbath, Queen, the Stone Roses, Oasis, Coldplay, Simple Minds, Robert Plant and Manic Street Preachers among others. Including contributions by Ozzy Osbourne, Liam Gallagher, Tony Iommi and Chris Martin

programme.title: Robert Plant: By Myself
programme.desc: The singer discusses his varied life and musical career, including his upbringing in the West Midlands town of Stourbridge, his rise to stardom as the singer of influential rock band Led Zeppelin and his solo projects. The programme also reflects on his collaboration with country singer Alison Krauss, with whom he made the Grammy-winning album Raising Sand, and his reunion with virtuoso guitarist Jimmy Page

programme.title: Coldplay in the BBC Horseshoe
programme.desc: A 2008 performance by the rock band on a specially built stage outside BBC Television Centre. They perform a selection of songs from their fourth studio album Viva La Vida or Death and All His Friends, alongside some of their greatest hits

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents the edition first broadcast on 14 December, featuring performances by Rob'n'Raz, Van Morrison and Cliff Richard, Madonna, Latino Rave, Wet Wet Wet and Tina Turner

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989

programme.title: This Farming Life

programme.title: BBC Proms 2020
programme.desc: A BBC Grand Virtual Orchestra made up of more than 300 BBC musicians perform Beethoveniana by composer Iain Farrington, a brand new reworking of Beethoven's nine symphonies commissioned by the BBC Proms to celebrate the opening of the 2020 Proms season

programme.title: BBC Proms Classics
programme.desc: Katie Derham presents this 2017 concert by the City of Birmingham Symphony Orchestra, conducted by Mirga Grazinyte-Tyla. The show features Beethoven's Fifth Symphony, Stravinsky's Violin Concerto with soloist Leila Josefowicz, and a piece by Gerald Barry, featuring tenor Allan Clayton

programme.title: The Merchant of Venice: Royal Shakespeare Company
programme.desc: When a contract between Bassanio and Shylock is broken in Venice, simmering racial tensions boil over. A wronged father, and despised outsider, Shylock looks to exact the ultimate price for a deal sealed in blood. Polly Findlay directs this RSC production of Shakespeare's uncompromising play, with Makram J Khoury, Patsy Ferran and Jamie Ballard

programme.title: Institute
programme.desc: In a shadowy establishment, residents attempt to create order from the chaos of life. Carers become patients, memories fracture and relationships collide. Drama by the physical theatre company Gecko, starring Chris Evans and Vanessa Guevara Flores

programme.title: Shakespeare in Italy
programme.desc: The historian concludes his tour of the country beginning with a visit to Venice, where he meets actor Ciaran Hinds to discuss why the Bard chose the city as the setting for The Merchant of Venice. Francesco is then joined by Mark Rylance in Rome as they explore how the playwright used the Italian capital for the plots of Julius Caesar and Antony and Cleopatra to avoid Elizabethan censors. Finally, on the island of Stromboli, off the northern coast of Sicily, Francesco uncovers the location for Shakespeare's late masterpiece - The Tempest

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Mark Radcliffe marks the final round of the FA Cup by providing football fans with a chance to revisit some of the best (and worst) songs produced to celebrate the tournament's conclusion over the years. Viewers at home will be able to make use of social media to vote for their favourite tunes, meaning that woeful ditties such as Manchester United's 1996 song Move Move Move, featuring a youthful Paul Scholes, David Beckham and the Neville brothers, could feature alongside the arguably more 'professional' sounding efforts of the London Community Gospel Choir, which sang Abide With Me - the FA Cup's traditional anthem - ahead of Chelsea's clash with Everton at Wembley in 2009

programme.title: All Over the Place: Asia
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and his CBBC mates go on an adventure to find the most unusual and amazing places in Asia. They discover a car museum in the middle of the desert in the United Arab Emirates; take a tour of a Samurai house in Japan; get plenty of exercise at a stepwell in India; head for China to taste a hot pot that includes cooked brains in its ingredients list and race down a river in traditional wooden washtubs in Japan! Ed is joined on this bonkers road trip by Hacker and Dodge, Victoria Cook, Iain Stirling, Johny Pitts, Chris Johnson and Naomi Wilkinson - there's stuff to do in Asia that is totally ace and it turns up All Over The Place!

programme.title: Show Me What You're Made Of

The kids are taken aback to discover that they are joining the cleaning team at a big bus garage in south London. It's hard physical work, and the shift goes on long into the night, requiring them to sweep, mop and wash each double-decker bus by hand. programme.desc: CBeebies' motorbike rider Grace shows off three machines that run on rails!

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: When Gran Gran and JoJo find a family recipe for Great Gran Gran's signature Saint Lucian banana bread, they're excited to bake a batch of it! But there's one problem - cake mix has been spilt on the recipe, so they don't know how long to bake it for!

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: It's the day after the Brooklyn-Bells' wedding and time to say goodbye to the puppy

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: Rory Crawford meets a dog who is visiting the vet to donate blood. Ferne Corrigan visits a wildlife park whose vet is checking on some ferrets

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Niger and Baby Bear meet babies Isobel, Idris, Lincoln, Amber, Amelia, Jason and Willow.

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Click Clack becomes a trainee kite pilot and learns how to navigate a kite

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: Mama Hen loses a needle lent to her by Eagle, so she pecks the ground searching for it

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures
programme.desc: When Jen accidentally grinds up some giraffe thorn tree seeds that Mr Hammond wanted Andy to plant, Andy must head to the Namibian desert in Southern Africa and track some down - and what better way to do that from the top of a giraffe itself? However, when another male giraffe arrives on the scene, a fight ensues and Andy is caught in the middle

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Moira can't decide which stallholder should win Hubber of the Year. Everyone has a suggestion, but Molly provides the solution to Moira's problem

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: On the day of the annual Five Sisters Run, John tells Katie Morag he can beat her in the children's hill race because boys are better than girls at sport, prompting Agnes to hatch a mischievous plan

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Sarah plans a surprise for when her hibernating tortoise wakes up

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: At her first school sports day, Lola decides to enter an egg and spoon race

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: The animated adventures of an energetic baby monkey and his long-suffering mother, who take a light-hearted approach to problem-solving and teamwork as they tackle life's challenges together

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Five objects of various shapes and colours visit the Clangers' planet. But what are they?

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Upsy Daisy finds Makka Pakka's uff-uff when it blows away and lands on her bed. She sets off through the garden to return it to its owner

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Helen George reads Held in Love, a story about a mother's hopes and dreams for her new-born baby!

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Julia Somerville, Gloria Hunniford and Angela Rippon report on builder-checking websites, in regard to how extensively those sites check out the traders they list. Plus, a couple's two-year battle with one of the biggest names in insurance

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a cemetery lodge in the Cumbria town of Maryport and a house in Neath Port Talbot, and Dion Dublin casts his eye over a commercial unit in Birmingham

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Gary Pe and Nick Hall help two teams look for items in the West Sussex town of Ardingly, while presenter Anita Manning investigates the history of funfairs

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Ash reacts badly to an insinuation, while the hospital ball sees one relationship flourish while another founders. Australian drama, starring Fiona Press and Mitchell Bourke

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton accompanies a couple on a North Yorkshire house hunt. They are calling time on running a guest house in the city and have a £650 000 budget to find a rural family home with business potential. Whilst in the county, Alistair also pays a visit to a heritage train line and helps the team to get their impressive locomotives fired up for the start of the tourist season

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Three talented artists to make something special for the homes of people they've never met. In Lincolnshire, a buyer is looking for a gift for her husband as a thank-you for all he does for the family and to mark his long service to charity work. Will a stone sculptor, a mosaic maker or an illustrator create the perfect present? Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades explores the First World War's legacy on the UK today, through archive and delving deeper into three barn fixes - some bagpipes, a soldier's wire cutters and two RAF mascots

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Gethin Jones present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Welsh international footballer Hal Robson-Kanu, British athletics great Colin Jackson, rugby league legend Paul Sculthorpe and world champion boxer Terri Harper join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Chef and restaurateur John Torode and food writer and ingredients expert Gregg Wallace judge the efforts of four remaining contestants battling to remain in the contest, tasking them to cook a faultless two-course meal. Lending their thoughts on proceedings are three legends of the series - former champions Phil Vickery, Lisa Faulkner and John Partridge. They know exactly what it takes to get through this competition and are almost certainly in for a treat as the contenders push the boat out in a bid to secure their semi-final place

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen rewinds the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television. This time, he's on a quest for sporting glory, which leads him into a world of barrel racing, hot pants and wrestling vicars - escapades full of lively characters from the nation's past

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian's sense of humour is put to the test when he is sent to the Brecon Beacons to take part in one of the British Army's most gruelling training exercises

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Armchair fan Jack turns his comedic sights on Britain's attitude towards its sporting heroes, examining the delight the nation takes in building its sports stars up, as well as bringing them crashing down again. From the red cards and free kicks of David Beckham to Andy Murray's difficult route to the top of tennis and Zola Budd's infamous 1984 Olympic tangle, Jack looks at why it doesn't matter how incredibly talented a person is - if they want to become a British hero, they are not in for an easy ride

programme.title: The Other One
programme.desc: Fed up with Cathy festering in her bed, post-wedding cancellation, and stalking Marcus and Meredith on what was supposed to be Cathy and Marcus' honeymoon, Cat decides the best thing to cheer up her half-sibling is to take her away on their own 'sister honeymoon'. She's got just the place in mind - their late Dad's favourite pub. At the solicitors, Marilyn and Tess learn that Colin's will contains an absolute bombshell and that they must pass on some shocking news to their daughters. Comedy, starring Ellie White, Lauren Socha, Siobhan Finneran and Rebecca Front

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share
programme.desc: Kayleigh has had enough of work and fancies a day off, but John is not having any of it - or is he? Co-written by and starring Peter Kay and Sian Gibson

programme.title: Olympics 2012
programme.desc: A look back at the show produced by Trainspotting and Slumdog Millionaire director Danny Boyle, which marked the start of the London 2012 Olympic Games. The ceremony reflected the key themes and priorities of the event, based on sport, inspiration, youth and urban transformation, and celebrated the contributions made by the UK to the world through innovation, as well as the creativity of British people

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Theo Randall and Shivi Ramoutar, and special guest Bill Bailey joins via video link. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Mary Berry

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond focus on wild food, cooking gnocchi with smoked haddock and wild greens, with chef Billy Boyter and crime writer Val McDermid

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents a look ahead to the weekend's football, including Arsenal v Manchester City and Manchester United v Chelsea in the FA Cup semi-finals

programme.title: Olympics Rewind
programme.desc: The first of four parts of a look back at the Games, including the exploits of cyclists Lizzie Armistead and Chris Hoy

programme.title: Olympics Rewind
programme.desc: Continuing the look back at the Games, focusing on 'Super Saturday', when Jessica Ennis-Hill, Mo Farah and Greg Rutherford all claimed athletics gold medals

programme.title: Scrum V Classics
programme.desc: A look back at France v Wales in round three of the 2005 Six Nations Championship. Wales travelled to Paris to face the defending champions, hoping to take another step toward their first Grand Slam since 1978

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen rewinds the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television. This time, he's on a quest for sporting glory, which leads him into a world of barrel racing, hot pants and wrestling vicars - escapades full of lively characters from the nation's past

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys hosts the celebrity version of the classic quiz. Actor Dylan Llewellyn, TV presenter Claire McCollum, digital content creator Caspar Lee, and Radio One DJ Rickie Haywood-Williams return to the spotlight, answering questions on specialist subjects Quentin Tarantino Films, 1980s Television, YouTube and Game of Thrones

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. Those standing behind the podiums this week are actors Michael Sheen, Steve Pemberton and Mark Benton, impressionist Jess Robinson, entertainers Anita Harris and Bobby Crush and former footballers Neil Ruddock and Steve McMahon

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: When Lev is called to help a patient, Evgeny, who has been stabbed, he is delighted to find out that he is Russian, and the pair bond over their similar heritage. However, he soon finds himself reflecting on disturbing and traumatic memories from his own past and his childhood in Russia. Meanwhile, Jan forgets to buy an anniversary gift for her wife, Ffion, and promise to make it up to her, but an emergency means that Jan has to put her work her relationship once more. Elsewhere, Charlie decides to take a step back

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. This time, Mo Gilligan and Anne-Marie pop by for a chat and a game of Musical VAR, and Jamie Redknapp takes on Dina Asher-Smith in The Sporting Fix. Plus, Zara Larsson performs in The Garden Sessions

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents highlights of the Premier League fixture between Norwich City and Burnley at Carrow Road

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Arsenal v Manchester City. Action from the opening semi-final at Wembley Stadium, where the teams battled for a place in the final. The Citizens came out on top in this season's Premier League encounters between the teams, winning 3-0 on both occasions, most recently on 17 June as football returned to action after the lockdown

programme.title: Mavericks: Sport's Lost Heroes
programme.desc: A profile of 1970s Formula One driver Tom Pryce, who showed great promise but died in tragic circumstances at the 1977 South African Grand Prix

programme.title: Short Term 12 (2013)
programme.desc: A counsellor at a foster care centre for troubled teenagers tries to help a gifted newcomer realise her talents and assists a 17-year-old in dealing with the prospect of having to leave the facility when he turns 18. However, helping them get a good start in life forces her to come to terms with her own difficult past. Drama, starring Brie Larson and John Gallagher Jr

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents highlights of the Premier League fixture between Norwich City and Burnley at Carrow Road

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Arsenal v Manchester City. Action from the opening semi-final at Wembley Stadium, where the teams battled for a place in the final. The Citizens came out on top in this season's Premier League encounters between the teams, winning 3-0 on both occasions, most recently on 17 June as football returned to action after the lockdown

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: All the latest political news from Wales and beyond, presented by James Williams

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Lynne Donnelly wants to move to Australia, so she takes her family for a trial week in Melbourne in the hope of convincing them to leave their home in Scotland for good

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Charles Hanson are in Oswestry, Shropshire, to help two teams look for items, while host Natasha Raskin Sharp has advice on collectibles from occupied Japan

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley finds out about the vicars of Eyam, Derbyshire, who initiated a lockdown 355 years ago to protect the rest of northern England from the bubonic plague

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore returns to transform more items reclaimed from a tip, beginning by rescuing a granite worktop from Altrincham Recycling Centre in Greater Manchester

programme.title: Olympics Rewind
programme.desc: The third part of a look back at the Games, focusing on the second half of the Games - a hectic eight days in which the British gold rush continued in east London

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin goes property hunting with a couple of new grandparents who have £800,000 to buy a home in Wiltshire close to their family. The presenter also takes time out to learn about the traditional process of curing Wiltshire bacon

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Tom Heap are in the Shropshire Hills to find out about an ambitious project to create a nature corridor between the two peaks of Long Mynd and Stiperstones. Tom meets a farmer who's become a convert to environmentally friendly regenerative farming, while Ellie goes in search of rare and much misunderstood wildlife. Charlotte Smith tests the strength of Britain's food supply system in the wake of the pandemic and with Brexit on the horizon

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manchester United v Chelsea (Kick-off 6.00pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the last-four match at Wembley Stadium. The sides last met here in the final of this competition two years ago, when Chelsea prevailed 1-0 courtesy of an Eden Hazard penalty. However, United are unbeaten in the six meetings between the clubs in all competitions and have defeated the Blues three times this season, completing a double over them in the league and winning at Stamford Bridge in the fourth round of the Carabao Cup

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the historic Buckfast Abbey in Devon, where experts examine an 18th-century tankard, an iron chest with a hidden lock dating back to the 1640s, London street signs, a Biba frock, and a piece of shrapnel from the battleship Bismarck

programme.title: The Luminaries
programme.desc: Anna is tried for murder, but Emery is still missing - along with her alibi - and Moody refuses to act on her behalf as he believes the case to be indefensible. However, due to a mix-up the lawyer ends up with Lauderback's trunk and discovers a series of letters from Crosbie that reveal a startling fact. Lydia hosts a seance to make contact with Emery, intending to implicate Anna further, but instead she channels the spirit of Sook and inadvertently accuses Carver of murder. Period drama, starring Eva Green

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Bournemouth v Southampton at the Vitality Stadium and Tottenham Hotspur v Leicester City at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium

programme.title: Looking for Alaska
programme.desc: A truce with the Weekday Warriors proves short-lived, Alaska sets Miles up on his very first date at the school's basketball game, and Takumi has a suspicion who the rat might be

programme.title: Looking for Alaska
programme.desc: Miles agrees to spend Thanksgiving with Alaska, but a trip to the Colonel's home for the holiday that was designed to bring everyone together only leaves the group further apart
