Charlotte Smith tests the strength of Britain's food supply system in the wake of the pandemic and with Brexit on the horizon

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manchester United v Chelsea (Kick-off 6.00pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the last-four match at Wembley Stadium. The sides last met here in the final of this competition two years ago, when Chelsea prevailed 1-0 courtesy of an Eden Hazard penalty. However, United are unbeaten in the six meetings between the clubs in all competitions and have defeated the Blues three times this season, completing a double over them in the league and winning at Stamford Bridge in the fourth round of the Carabao Cup

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the historic Buckfast Abbey in Devon, where experts examine an 18th-century tankard, an iron chest with a hidden lock dating back to the 1640s, London street signs, a Biba frock, and a piece of shrapnel from the battleship Bismarck

programme.title: The Luminaries
programme.desc: Anna is tried for murder, but Emery is still missing - along with her alibi - and Moody refuses to act on her behalf as he believes the case to be indefensible. However, due to a mix-up the lawyer ends up with Lauderback's trunk and discovers a series of letters from Crosbie that reveal a startling fact. Lydia hosts a seance to make contact with Emery, intending to implicate Anna further, but instead she channels the spirit of Sook and inadvertently accuses Carver of murder. Period drama, starring Eva Green

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Bournemouth v Southampton at the Vitality Stadium and Tottenham Hotspur v Leicester City at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium

programme.title: Looking for Alaska
programme.desc: A truce with the Weekday Warriors proves short-lived, Alaska sets Miles up on his very first date at the school's basketball game, and Takumi has a suspicion who the rat might be

programme.title: Looking for Alaska
programme.desc: Miles agrees to spend Thanksgiving with Alaska, but a trip to the Colonel's home for the holiday that was designed to bring everyone together only leaves the group further apart

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Julia Somerville, Gloria Hunniford and Angela Rippon report on builder-checking websites, in regard to how extensively those sites check out the traders they list. Plus, a couple's two-year battle with one of the biggest names in insurance

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a cemetery lodge in the Cumbria town of Maryport and a house in Neath Port Talbot, and Dion Dublin casts his eye over a commercial unit in Birmingham

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A family's horrific ordeal when masked thieves burst into its home. Plus, how a bungling bike thief looking to steal a new set of wheels ended getting a ride in a police car

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Gary Pe and Nick Hall help two teams look for items in the West Sussex town of Ardingly, while presenter Anita Manning investigates the history of funfairs

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Ash reacts badly to an insinuation, while the hospital ball sees one relationship flourish while another founders. Australian drama, starring Fiona Press and Mitchell Bourke

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton accompanies a couple on a North Yorkshire house hunt. They are calling time on running a guest house in the city and have a £650 000 budget to find a rural family home with business potential. Whilst in the county, Alistair also pays a visit to a heritage train line and helps the team to get their impressive locomotives fired up for the start of the tourist season

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Three talented artists to make something special for the homes of people they've never met. In Lincolnshire, a buyer is looking for a gift for her husband as a thank-you for all he does for the family and to mark his long service to charity work. Will a stone sculptor, a mosaic maker or an illustrator create the perfect present? Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades explores the First World War's legacy on the UK today, through archive and delving deeper into three barn fixes - some bagpipes, a soldier's wire cutters and two RAF mascots

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Gethin Jones present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Welsh international footballer Hal Robson-Kanu, British athletics great Colin Jackson, rugby league legend Paul Sculthorpe and world champion boxer Terri Harper join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Chef and restaurateur John Torode and food writer and ingredients expert Gregg Wallace judge the efforts of four remaining contestants battling to remain in the contest, tasking them to cook a faultless two-course meal. Lending their thoughts on proceedings are three legends of the series - former champions Phil Vickery, Lisa Faulkner and John Partridge. They know exactly what it takes to get through this competition and are almost certainly in for a treat as the contenders push the boat out in a bid to secure their semi-final place

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Armchair fan Jack turns his comedic sights on Britain's attitude towards its sporting heroes, examining the delight the nation takes in building its sports stars up, as well as bringing them crashing down again. From the red cards and free kicks of David Beckham to Andy Murray's difficult route to the top of tennis and Zola Budd's infamous 1984 Olympic tangle, Jack looks at why it doesn't matter how incredibly talented a person is - if they want to become a British hero, they are not in for an easy ride

programme.title: Keepin 'er Country
programme.desc: Documentary series following the stories of Northern Ireland's country music enthusiasts. This edition focuses on Ireland's biggest truck show, meeting the lorry-loving women who spend hundreds of thousands on their vehicles to keep them in tip-top condition, and the men who combine their weekly workload with pulling up to country music festivals at the weekends

programme.title: The Other One
programme.desc: Fed up with Cathy festering in her bed, post-wedding cancellation, and stalking Marcus and Meredith on what was supposed to be Cathy and Marcus' honeymoon, Cat decides the best thing to cheer up her half-sibling is to take her away on their own 'sister honeymoon'. She's got just the place in mind - their late Dad's favourite pub. At the solicitors, Marilyn and Tess learn that Colin's will contains an absolute bombshell and that they must pass on some shocking news to their daughters. Comedy, starring Ellie White, Lauren Socha, Siobhan Finneran and Rebecca Front

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share
programme.desc: Kayleigh has had enough of work and fancies a day off, but John is not having any of it - or is he? Co-written by and starring Peter Kay and Sian Gibson

programme.title: Olympics 2012
programme.desc: A look back at the show produced by Trainspotting and Slumdog Millionaire director Danny Boyle, which marked the start of the London 2012 Olympic Games. The ceremony reflected the key themes and priorities of the event, based on sport, inspiration, youth and urban transformation, and celebrated the contributions made by the UK to the world through innovation, as well as the creativity of British people

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Theo Randall and Shivi Ramoutar, and special guest Bill Bailey joins via video link. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Mary Berry

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond focus on wild food, cooking gnocchi with smoked haddock and wild greens, with chef Billy Boyter and crime writer Val McDermid

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents a look ahead to the weekend's football, including Arsenal v Manchester City and Manchester United v Chelsea in the FA Cup semi-finals

programme.title: Olympics Rewind
programme.desc: The first of four parts of a look back at the Games, including the exploits of cyclists Lizzie Armistead and Chris Hoy

programme.title: Olympics Rewind
programme.desc: Continuing the look back at the Games, focusing on 'Super Saturday', when Jessica Ennis-Hill, Mo Farah and Greg Rutherford all claimed athletics gold medals

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In the suburbs of Bristol, Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich turn a long, pointed front garden into a water-saving, wildlife-friendly space

programme.title: Maleficent (2014)
programme.desc: A fairy falls in love with an ambitious human nobleman, who betrays her in pursuit of claiming the throne. She swears to get revenge on him by placing a curse on his daughter, only to find herself developing a maternal bond with the girl. Fantasy adventure based on the story of Sleeping Beauty, starring Angelina Jolie, Elle Fanning, Sharlto Copley and Imelda Staunton

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys hosts the celebrity version of the classic quiz. Actor Dylan Llewellyn, TV presenter Claire McCollum, digital content creator Caspar Lee, and Radio One DJ Rickie Haywood-Williams return to the spotlight, answering questions on specialist subjects Quentin Tarantino Films, 1980s Television, YouTube and Game of Thrones

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. Those standing behind the podiums this week are actors Michael Sheen, Steve Pemberton and Mark Benton, impressionist Jess Robinson, entertainers Anita Harris and Bobby Crush and former footballers Neil Ruddock and Steve McMahon

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: When Lev is called to help a patient, Evgeny, who has been stabbed, he is delighted to find out that he is Russian, and the pair bond over their similar heritage. However, he soon finds himself reflecting on disturbing and traumatic memories from his own past and his childhood in Russia. Meanwhile, Jan forgets to buy an anniversary gift for her wife, Ffion, and promise to make it up to her, but an emergency means that Jan has to put her work her relationship once more. Elsewhere, Charlie decides to take a step back

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. This time, Mo Gilligan and Anne-Marie pop by for a chat and a game of Musical VAR, and Jamie Redknapp takes on Dina Asher-Smith in The Sporting Fix. Plus, Zara Larsson performs in The Garden Sessions

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents highlights of the Premier League fixture between Norwich City and Burnley at Carrow Road

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Arsenal v Manchester City. Action from the opening semi-final at Wembley Stadium, where the teams battled for a place in the final. The Citizens came out on top in this season's Premier League encounters between the teams, winning 3-0 on both occasions, most recently on 17 June as football returned to action after the lockdown

programme.title: Hot Property
programme.desc: Filly meets his match with eccentric Adam, a louder-than-life student who loves to sing about gonorrhoea

programme.title: Short Term 12 (2013)
programme.desc: A counsellor at a foster care centre for troubled teenagers tries to help a gifted newcomer realise her talents and assists a 17-year-old in dealing with the prospect of having to leave the facility when he turns 18. However, helping them get a good start in life forces her to come to terms with her own difficult past. Drama, starring Brie Larson and John Gallagher Jr

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Lynne Donnelly wants to move to Australia, so she takes her family for a trial week in Melbourne in the hope of convincing them to leave their home in Scotland for good

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Charles Hanson are in Oswestry, Shropshire, to help two teams look for items, while host Natasha Raskin Sharp has advice on collectibles from occupied Japan

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley finds out about the vicars of Eyam, Derbyshire, who initiated a lockdown 355 years ago to protect the rest of northern England from the bubonic plague

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore returns to transform more items reclaimed from a tip, beginning by rescuing a granite worktop from Altrincham Recycling Centre in Greater Manchester

programme.title: Olympics Rewind
programme.desc: The third part of a look back at the Games, focusing on the second half of the Games - a hectic eight days in which the British gold rush continued in east London

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin goes property hunting with a couple of new grandparents who have £800,000 to buy a home in Wiltshire close to their family. The presenter also takes time out to learn about the traditional process of curing Wiltshire bacon

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Guest panellists engage in topical debate and celebrity interviews from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson hosts, as people in Bradford bring in their treasures and collectibles for the Real Deal team. Tim Hogarth is keen to show the other dealers a thing or two as this is his home town, but a quad amp leaves him floundering. He is, however, keen to get his hands on a Corgi bulldozer for his grandson. Meanwhile, Cheryl Hakeney falls head over heels for a terracotta dog

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Friends from Taunton in Somerset answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Quiz, hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Nate and Rhona try to calm Cain as Kim threatens to bring down Butlers Farm, while Jamie feels trapped as Andrea holds all the cards. Nicola slyly railroads Jimmy into suggesting that they buy into the cafe, and Belle's heart is broken as her hopes are crushed

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin asks Abi to move in with him and Jack, but, while she is delighted, Seb is angry that she has moved on so quickly from her own kids to Kevin's. Geoff promises Yasmeen that when she comes home, things will be different and he'll seek professional help, while the factory girls are shocked when they realise Carla is once more their boss

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: When a body is dredged up from a slurry pit on a remote farm, the investigation forces owner Danny Pryor to admit he has a number of illegal Serbian workers on the payroll. A faded tattoo identifies the victim as Jack Reeves, a young man from the local travelling community, but what is his connection to the immigrants? As the case unfolds, Vera and her team must connect the dots between Jack and a wounded Serbian, and expose the tragic truth at the heart of Pryor Farm. With Mark Bonnar, Mark Womack and Jim Sturgeon

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Barry Gibb reflects on his life and 50-year career as a songwriter and singer, explaining how he has finally managed to come to terms with being the last surviving member of the Bee Gees. The musician recalls how the group rose from child performers to global superstars in the wake of the Saturday Night Fever soundtrack in the late 1970s. He also reveals how a childhood accident nearly killed him, what really happened when he and his future wife sneaked into Dr Who's Tardis, and gives his verdict when Piers serenades him with his falsetto voice

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: More mystery celebrities battle it out in a singing contest while dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes, while a panel of more famous faces tries to guess their identities. Nick Cannon oversees the contest, as mystery singers dressed as Rottweiler, Ladybug, Tree, Penguin, Flower and Fox take to the stage, while panellists Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Nicole Scherzinger, and regular star of the British spin-off Ken Jeong try to work out who is behind the mask

programme.title: 1000 Heartbeats
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the quiz show in which contestants' pulses are monitored and they must complete seven rounds before their hearts beat 1,000 times to win £25,000 - meaning they will have to stay calm under pressure or risk losing the jackpot

programme.desc: Fizz and Weasel shoot people with toy arrows to make them fall in love with Fizz. But Magoo mistakes them for a giant hornet.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: This time, the chef travels to south Wales, where he is accompanied by Michelin-starred chef Stephen Terry. James cooks a Welsh lamb ragu on a beautiful mountainside, and samples award-winning Welsh wine and organic Welsh chocolate. Later, he visits Shaun Hill at his renowned restaurant, The Walnut Tree

programme.title: Small Soldiers (1998)
programme.desc: A hi-tech computer chip has a bizarre effect on a squad of toy soldiers, causing mayhem for their owner as the miniature marauders engage in a series of scaled-down skirmishes. Children's adventure mixing live action and computer animation, starring Kirsten Dunst, Denis Leary and Gregory Smith, with the voices of Tommy Lee Jones, Frank Langella, Sarah Michelle Gellar and Christina Ricci

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: More mystery celebrities battle it out in a singing contest while dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes, while a panel of more famous faces tries to guess their identities. Nick Cannon oversees the contest, as mystery singers dressed as Black Widow, Thingamajig, Butterfly, Leopard and Flamingo perform, while judges Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Ken Jeong and Nicole Scherzinger try to guess their identity

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Thousands of pounds in prize-money donations to charity are up for grabs once again, as Bradley Walsh invites TV presenter Jamie Theakston, singer-songwriter and actress Faye Tozer, retired England rugby player Ben Cohen and comedienne Sara Pascoe to take part in the quiz. To win, they must work as a team to prove their general knowledge know-how by beating one of the show's resident experts at their own game

programme.title: Ninja Warrior UK
programme.desc: Competitors from across the nation test their strength, speed and courage in this obstacle-course challenge, hosted by Ben Shephard, Rochelle Humes and Chris Kamara. Those who make it to the end of the adrenaline-fuelled mission face one final task - scaling the heights of a tower named Mount Midoriyama - as they battle it out for the title of Ninja Warrior UK

programme.title: The Voice Kids
programme.desc: Emma Willis presents the junior version of the singing talent contest, giving youngsters a chance to achieve stardom. The Voice UK's Will.i.am returns as one of the four coaches, with McFly's Danny Jones and fellow pop stars Pixie Lott and Paloma Faith occupying the other spinning red chairs. Youngsters between the ages of seven and 14 display their musical talents in a bid to win a £30,000 prize to contribute to their musical education, along with a family holiday to Universal Orlando Resort

programme.title: Thunderball (1965)
programme.desc: James Bond travels to the Bahamas on the trail of two nuclear warheads stolen from a British aircraft. The lethal weapons have fallen into the hands of international crime syndicate Spectre, which plans to fire them at the US coast unless an exorbitant ransom is paid. Spy adventure, starring Sean Connery, Claudine Auger, Adolfo Celi and Lois Maxwell

programme.title: Play to the Whistle
programme.desc: Alan Shearer, James Anderson and Jimmy Carr join team captains Frank Lampard and Bradley Walsh for the sports-based comedy panel show, hosted by Holly Willoughby. Funnymen Romesh Ranganathan and Seann Walsh lend a hand with the laughs

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The bumbling blockhead is mistaken for an escaped convict and finds himself behind bars - where he soon makes himself at home while the real baddie has an awful time in the hands of Bean's landlady

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Having learned Master Wu is the 'Treacherous One,' the ninja find themselves under siege at the Monastery, trying to protect Wu from the wrath of Aspheera.

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Ben and Gwen can't wait for the Omni-Copter to arrive

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: Martin and Roman Kemp present the Sunday morning entertainment show, featuring music, chat and a host of fun and games. They are joined by Roman's I'm a Celebrity buddy Myles Stephenson, singer-songwriter Anne-Marie and operatic star Katherine Jenkins

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: The chef is joined by Brian Turner to explore the culinary delights of his native Yorkshire, with James visiting Frances Atkins' restaurant The Yorke Arms, where he enjoys her signature dish al fresco in her unusual dining room the Onion. Brian tastes the delights of some local charcuterie and is challenged to some off-road driving, while James brushes up his clay-pigeon shooting skills

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare pork belly served with Lisa's potato salad and a salsa verde, vegetarian stuffed squash, and chocolate and coconut pie

programme.title: Beautiful Baking with Juliet Sear
programme.desc: The baking expert creates a spread for a winter themed party with the help of DJ Dev Griffin, who learns how to make Juliet's winter woodland mushroom cake. Other menu highlights are meringue lollies, s'mores cupcakes, pigs in blankets and filo pastry pies

programme.title: The Voice Kids
programme.desc: Emma Willis presents the junior version of the singing talent contest, giving youngsters a chance to achieve stardom. The Voice UK's Will.i.am returns as one of the four coaches, with McFly's Danny Jones and fellow pop stars Pixie Lott and Paloma Faith occupying the other spinning red chairs. Youngsters between the ages of seven and 14 display their musical talents in a bid to win a £30,000 prize to contribute to their musical education, along with a family holiday to Universal Orlando Resort

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: More mystery celebrities battle it out in a singing contest while dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes, while a panel of more famous faces tries to guess their identities. Nick Cannon oversees the contest, as mystery singers dressed as Black Widow, Thingamajig, Butterfly, Leopard and Flamingo perform, while judges Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Ken Jeong and Nicole Scherzinger try to guess their identity

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: In this special episode, Paul returns to Battersea Dogs & Cats Home to see how staff coped during one of the most extraordinary and dramatic periods in its history - the coronavirus pandemic. Before Battersea closes its doors to the public for the first time in 160 years, Paul spends one last day at the centre helping in whatever way he can, as the team goes all out against the clock to rehome more than 150 dogs and cats, puppies and kittens. Paul offers to foster a dog, but when he meets an adorable litter of puppies - and one crawls into his coat - can he hold his resolve, or will she become a permanent member of the O'Grady family?

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: Jason Manford narrates a celebration of Weatherfield's most memorable weddings, of which there have been 130 weddings over the decades. Viewers will get to the chance to see some of the most dramatic, shocking and heart-warming occasions, including the big days of Raquel and Curly, Shelley and Charlie, and Beth and Kirk

programme.title: Diamonds Are Forever (1971)
programme.desc: James Bond investigates a diamond-smuggling operation, and discovers that behind the scheme is his insidious arch-enemy Blofeld, who is planning to construct a devastating orbital doomsday weapon powered by the precious stones, which will force world powers to disarm and give him global domination. Spy adventure, starring Sean Connery, Jill St John, Charles Gray, Bernard Lee and Lois Maxwell

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Margo takes great pride in her new job at the surgery, but after a chance encounter with a recluse, she begins to realise where her greatest talent lies. Louisa grows closer to Sven, but as Gerry befriends a convict and Leslie begins spending all his time in the local bars, she begins to suspect she has made a mistake bringing her children to Corfu

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Mary's past catches up with her when an unwelcome visitor delivers an ultimatum, as secrets and rifts threaten the unity of the family. News about the running of Downton Hospital puts Violet on the warpath, while those below stairs find social changes are putting their futures in jeopardy and Daisy's good intentions at the Mallerton House auction have disastrous consequences. Final series of the costume drama, starring Maggie Smith, Hugh Bonneville and Michelle Dockery

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Plans for the wedding are gathering steam, but Lady Mary's meddling lands Carson in trouble with Mrs Hughes. Cora and Violet clash over plans for the hospital takeover, and Thomas's job search is proving disheartening. Mary comes up with a fresh idea that offers Anna new hope, prompting a trip to London, and Lady Edith faces challenges at the magazine although it's a problem closer to home that reaches breaking point. A day out for the family and servants ends in panic and leaves Robert facing a difficult decision

programme.title: The Comedy Years
programme.desc: A look at comedy in 2003, when Tim, Lee and Dawn's love triangle reached a memorable conclusion in The Office and the original run of Cold Feet bowed out with a tragic storyline. It was also the year when members of the public found themselves the unwitting comedy stooges of hidden-camera shows like Trigger Happy TV and Three Non Blondes, Bruce Forsyth made a memorable appearance as guest host of Have I Got News for You and comedy legend Bob Monkhouse performed his last stand-up gig to an audience of fellow comics in a small London pub

programme.title: Last Laugh in Vegas
programme.desc: After adjusting to life in Nevada, and trying to impress flamboyant producer Frank Marino, Cannon and Ball, Anita Harris, Kenny Lynch, Bernie Clifton and Su Pollard take to the stage for their big gig in Las Vegas. With Jess Conrad, Mick Miller and Bobby Crush also hoping to wow the masses, it remains to be seen whether Frank's hard work has paid off

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Guest panellists engage in topical debate and celebrity interviews from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson hosts, as people in Bradford bring in their treasures and collectibles for the Real Deal team. Tim Hogarth is keen to show the other dealers a thing or two as this is his home town, but a quad amp leaves him floundering. He is, however, keen to get his hands on a Corgi bulldozer for his grandson. Meanwhile, Cheryl Hakeney falls head over heels for a terracotta dog

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Friends from Taunton in Somerset answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Quiz, hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Nate and Rhona try to calm Cain as Kim threatens to bring down Butlers Farm, while Jamie feels trapped as Andrea holds all the cards. Nicola slyly railroads Jimmy into suggesting that they buy into the cafe, and Belle's heart is broken as her hopes are crushed

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin asks Abi to move in with him and Jack, but, while she is delighted, Seb is angry that she has moved on so quickly from her own kids to Kevin's. Geoff promises Yasmeen that when she comes home, things will be different and he'll seek professional help, while the factory girls are shocked when they realise Carla is once more their boss

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: When a body is dredged up from a slurry pit on a remote farm, the investigation forces owner Danny Pryor to admit he has a number of illegal Serbian workers on the payroll. A faded tattoo identifies the victim as Jack Reeves, a young man from the local travelling community, but what is his connection to the immigrants? As the case unfolds, Vera and her team must connect the dots between Jack and a wounded Serbian, and expose the tragic truth at the heart of Pryor Farm. With Mark Bonnar, Mark Womack and Jim Sturgeon

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Barry Gibb reflects on his life and 50-year career as a songwriter and singer, explaining how he has finally managed to come to terms with being the last surviving member of the Bee Gees. The musician recalls how the group rose from child performers to global superstars in the wake of the Saturday Night Fever soundtrack in the late 1970s. He also reveals how a childhood accident nearly killed him, what really happened when he and his future wife sneaked into Dr Who's Tardis, and gives his verdict when Piers serenades him with his falsetto voice

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: More mystery celebrities battle it out in a singing contest while dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes, while a panel of more famous faces tries to guess their identities. Nick Cannon oversees the contest, as mystery singers dressed as Rottweiler, Ladybug, Tree, Penguin, Flower and Fox take to the stage, while panellists Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Nicole Scherzinger, and regular star of the British spin-off Ken Jeong try to work out who is behind the mask

programme.title: 1000 Heartbeats
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the quiz show in which contestants' pulses are monitored and they must complete seven rounds before their hearts beat 1,000 times to win £25,000 - meaning they will have to stay calm under pressure or risk losing the jackpot

programme.title: Tomb Raider (2018)
programme.desc: After her father's disappearance, kickboxing heiress Lara Croft makes money as a bike courier and refuses to claim her inheritance, convinced her dad is still alive. Determined to find him, she sets out across the sea to Hong Kong - but soon crosses paths with a small army of mercenaries determined to break into a supposedly haunted tomb. Action adventure based on the video game, starring Alicia Vikander, Dominic West, Walton Goggins and Kristin Scott Thomas

Determined to find him, she sets out across the sea to Hong Kong - but soon crosses paths with a small army of mercenaries determined to break into a supposedly haunted tomb. Action adventure based on the video game, starring Alicia Vikander, Dominic West, Walton Goggins and Kristin Scott Thomas

programme.desc: Carter has a heart attack and lapses into a deep coma, so Peter takes over the running of his company Pewterschmidt Industries, but becomes power-mad and ends up firing every member of the board of directors

programme.desc: A barman from rural inn the Maid in Splendour is shot dead as he walks near a ruined cottage in woodlands - and Barnaby's investigation reveals a clash between charming landlord Michael Bannerman, the unofficial squire of Midsomer Worthy, and his abrasive son Stephen, who now runs the pub. John Nettles and John Hopkins star, with guest appearances by William Gaunt, Alan Cox, Dave Hill and Frances Tomelty

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An informant reveals two local hoodlums are planning to raid a shipment of gold bullion from Haworth Point, prompting Shiner to set up an ambush to catch the would-be thieves. Gina receives a visit from her niece, who has fallen in with a bad crowd back in Manchester, while Liz enlists David to paint the surgery and sitting room - but is less than pleased with the results. Drama, starring Simon Molloy and Tricia Penrose

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank is embarrassed by his behaviour towards Tina. Biff wants to know why Dave is rushing into marriage. The Dingles hope to clean up selling Christmas stock in the village

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak is not happy in his role as Santa, Kathy and Dave's wedding day arrives, and Vic catches Viv and Terry sharing a romantic moment together

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Josie applies for a job as office manager with Mike. Andy tries to get Steve to see that Liz as much to blame as Jim. Fiona throws a grand opening party for her salon

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim returns from Ireland determined to put things right with Liz, but she refuses to see him. Maureen is annoyed when Reg calls off visiting her at the weekend

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth is called to Gossington Hall by her old friend Dolly Bantry (Joanna Lumley) when she finds the corpse of an unknown blonde in the library of her home. It soon transpires that there is no shortage of suspects, including local bad boy Basil Blake (Ben Miller) and Dolly's husband Arthur (James Fox), but when the victim is identified as a dancer in line to inherit a fortune, enquiries take a different turn. Crime drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Simon Callow, Jack Davenport and Tara Fitzgerald

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An informant reveals two local hoodlums are planning to raid a shipment of gold bullion from Haworth Point, prompting Shiner to set up an ambush to catch the would-be thieves. Gina receives a visit from her niece, who has fallen in with a bad crowd back in Manchester, while Liz enlists David to paint the surgery and sitting room - but is less than pleased with the results. Drama, starring Simon Molloy and Tricia Penrose

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The doctor spends his last day in Portwenn seeing an influx of patients as he prepares to close down the surgery and move to London. As he bids farewell to old friends and sets off to leave the village, he sees Louisa's teaching assistant Tasha fall from the Harbour Day stage and stops to help. But the bigger hold-up comes when pregnant Louisa is involved in a car accident. Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz star

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The curmudgeonly GP struggles to come to terms with his new role as a father, having planned to resume his career as a consultant in London before the arrival of his and Louisa's as-yet unnamed son. To make matters worse, he begins to have serious doubts about the competency of his replacement Dr Di Dibbs when she misdiagnoses a patient as well as herself. Martin is then dealt a devastating personal blow after hearing his aunt Joan has died. Starring Martin Clunes, Joanna Scanlan and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin reopen a 25-year-old case when the bones of a young boy are found buried inside the wall of a communal basement. The victim's former best friend takes part in therapy to revisit her memory of the night he disappeared and reveals a shocking secret, but Phillips and Steel worry her claims will not convince a jury. Guest starring Keith Barron and Colin Salmon, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Freema Agyeman and Ben Daniels

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet moves a step closer to tracking down Veronica's killer while investigating a recent murder that bears similarities to the cold case. Rachel finds out about an affair Nick once had with a juror in the middle of a trial - a revelation that could end his career. Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp, Suranne Jones and Rupert Graves

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack learns he is tipped for promotion with the uniform branch and even more flattered to discover he has a secret admirer, but before he has time to bask in this rare moment of glory a child murder claims his attention. Linking the disappearance of a 10-year-old boy to a mysterious hit-and-run killing, he is soon mired in a complex investigation and fuming about a bureaucratic blunder that leaves a known paedophile walking free on his patch. David Jason stars

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is asked to investigate the disappearance of a bank's most valued customer, who had been planning to sell the chairman a map showing where to find a long-lost silver mine. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster teams up with a dashing man to investigate a series of poison-pen letters in the sleepy village of Lymstock. However, Miss Marple suspects there is more to these deaths than meets the eye. programme.desc: Yasmeen is stunned by Geoff's latest move, while Imran tentatively broaches the subject of fostering with Nick and explains how they've put their plans on hold so as not to upset Leanne. programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alya fears that Geoff may win Yasmeen back and Kevin urges Abi to meet up with the twins one last time before they leave for Australia or she will live to regret it. Carla calls in at the factory and admits to Nick that she's bored of pulling pints and feels it's time she got back into the business, while Adam tells Gary it's obvious he's involved in some dodgy deal

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin asks Abi to move in with him and Jack, but, while she is delighted, Seb is angry that she has moved on so quickly from her own kids to Kevin's. Geoff promises Yasmeen that when she comes home, things will be different and he'll seek professional help, while the factory girls are shocked when they realise Carla is once more their boss

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A well-known eccentric contacts the Belgian sleuth when he is haunted by suicidal nightmares. However, he fails to follow Poirot's advice, and is found dead soon afterwards. Drama, starring David Suchet, with Joely Richardson and Mary Tamm

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Grizzled copper Jack Frost teams up with an obnoxious, chain-smoking colleague to investigate a seemingly straightforward hit-and-run incident that proves to have devastating repercussions for the victim's family and their peaceful rural community. David Jason, Bruce Alexander and Philip Jackson star

programme.title: The Great St Trinian's Train Robbery (1966)
programme.desc: A crook and his cronies stash the money from a train heist in a deserted old mansion, planning to recover it in good time. However, several years later, when the thieves arrive to collect their loot, they discover the building has been turned into a school for girls where the pupils have become infamous for their escapades. Comedy, starring Frankie Howerd, Dora Bryan, Reg Varney and George Cole

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster meets fellow enthusiasts Tommy and Tuppence at a nursing home, where the ramblings of an elderly resident prompt them to join forces and investigate the unsolved mystery of a murdered child. The trail leads to the Norfolk village of Farrell St Edmund, a community harbouring sinister secrets and tales of heartbreak. Geraldine McEwan heads an all-star cast, including Greta Scacchi, Anthony Andrews, Charles Dance, June Whitfield and Claire Bloom

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The launch of the latest sparkling wine by Midsomer Vinae Winery falls flat when critic Nadia Simons gives it a scathing review, then guests sampling the vintage start to collapse. Owner William Carnarvon suspects the Farmers' Wives Association of trying to destroy his business, while his wife Diana frets over her mother Matilda, one of the victims. Pathologist Kate reveals the drink was laced with slug poison, but how is the attack connected to the death of a child in a hit-and-run? Barnaby and Nelson are called in to investigate. Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee star in the mystery drama, with Claire Bloom, Mark Bonnar, Rosie Cavaliero and Ruth Gemmell

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A land dispute escalates during a cowboy fair run by the Wild West Society in the village of Ford Florey. Farmers Jack Fincher and Silas Burbage both claim to own a patch of wasteland known as the Swamp, but Dan Malko wants to buy it and create a travellers' site. Barnaby and Jones are called in when the feud leads to murder during the Wild West-style shoot-outs at the annual event. Guest starring Kenneth Cranham, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of Midsomer resident and biscuit factory boss Eric Calder leads Barnaby and Nelson to Copenhagen. The pair join forces with local detectives Birgitte Poulsen and Anna Degn in the hunt for the killer, and during the course of their investigation they discover the businessman had numerous links to the capital. Anglo-Danish collaboration originally shown to mark Midsomer's 100th episode, guest starring Ann Eleonora Jorgensen (The Killing), Birgitte Hjort Sorensen (Borgen), Sanjeev Bhaskar and Joanna Scanlan, alongside Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The evacuation of a central London hospital leads to the discovery of a tragic death. When Brooks and Devlin investigate, a suspect is arrested but the prosecutors (Ben Daniels and Freema Agyeman) face an uphill struggle building a case - so they team up with the detectives in a bid to gather more evidence. Patrick Malahide and Bill Paterson co-star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin fail to find a lead when a 13-year-old is kicked to death at Euston Station, so they turn to Chandler, who holds a press conference. As the detectives are led through a tangled web of family loyalties, the suspect's barrister, an old flame of Steel's, gathers evidence for an audacious defence strategy that could change the nature of the British legal system. Dervla Kirwan guest stars, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A former vice cop is found beaten to death, setting Brooks and Devlin off on an investigation through London's seedy underbelly. The victim's widow and his former boss both seem to know more about the murder than they are letting on - but the trail eventually leads to a respectable children's clothes shop. Crime drama, with guest appearances by Sean Pertwee (Cold Feet), Lesley Manville (Cranford) and Juliet Aubrey (Primeval)

programme.title: Barging Round Britain with John Sergeant
programme.desc: The broadcaster returns to travel more of Britain's canal network, exploring the extraordinary stories of the people and places on the way. The series starts with John navigating the Peak Forest Canal, which runs through stunning countryside from Buxworth in Derbyshire to Ashton-under-Lyne. Along the way, in Hyde he meets former boxer Ricky Hatton, who discusses his plans to revitalise the area and offer new opportunities to local youngsters, and joins a team of junior archaeologists at Mellor Mill, who are attempting to unearth the remains of what was once one of the world's largest mills

programme.desc: Ida is adamant she's not available for work when the employment agency gets in touch _ until she takes a call

Harriet rejects Will's suggestion that they attend a wedding fair. while Amelia is furious when she opens a 'get well soon' card to Dan from Brenda

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jamie worries about keeping the truth from Kim, but Andrea tells him they must convince her that they are back together for good. Moira lays unconscious in hospital, and Cain talks to the nurse about her condition, anxious he may lose her, while Bob is adamant he can run the cafe while Brenda is away

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Nate and Rhona try to calm Cain as Kim threatens to bring down Butlers Farm, while Jamie feels trapped as Andrea holds all the cards. Nicola slyly railroads Jimmy into suggesting that they buy into the cafe, and Belle's heart is broken as her hopes are crushed

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack is asked to investigate the theft of a car belonging to a friend of Mullett, but misjudges the grizzled Superintendent's capacity for tact and diplomacy and it is not long before he is taken off the case. Things take a shocking turn when the car finally turns up - with the body of a drug dealer in the boot. Detective drama, starring David Jason, with Melanie Thaw and Bruce Alexander

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A blizzard forces the sleuthing spinster to spend a night in isolated Sittaford House, situated on a Devon moor. Without explanation, the building's owner, up-and-coming politician Clive Trevelyan, decides to spend the evening in a local hotel - only for a Ouija board to predict his murder. Mystery drama with an all-star cast, including Geraldine McEwan, Robert Hardy, Matthew Kelly, Patricia Hodge and Mel Smith

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour detective Jack Frost investigates a woman's near-fatal shooting. The victim was all set to testify against a notorious drugs baron accused of murdering one of his employees, but the appearance of a contract killer makes her think again - and it appears someone on Jack's team is leaking information. Meanwhile, the station is turned upside down in the hunt for a missing lottery ticket. David Jason, Michael McKell and Bruce Alexander star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper detective accepts an invitation to spend yuletide at the house of an elderly millionaire who seems intent on provoking his money-grubbing sons into murdering him. At first he tries hard to ignore the strained atmosphere and relax - but soon finds himself with a gruesome mystery on his hands. Agatha Christie whodunit, starring David Suchet and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: At the Luxton Deeping Crime Festival, the unveiling of a newly discovered novel by deceased Midsomer crime writer George Summersbee is jeopardised when the manuscript is stolen and local artist Suzie Colebrooke is electrocuted by a booby-trapped roulette wheel. Can new father Barnaby untangle a web of jealousy and obsession to find the killer? With Fiona Dolman, Tamzin Malleson, Liberty Mills, Una Stubbs and Georgia Taylor

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Seventy-year-old Susan Webb is desperate find the daughter she gave up for adoption in 1968. At the time, she was suffering with depression, and after being treated with electroconvulsive therapy that severely affected her memory, she was left with no recollections of her child. Meanwhile, Sonia Walsh looks for the mother that abandoned her at the age of three with her younger sister. During her investigations, she discovered her mother had also given up another baby, and she is now determined to make up for the pain of the past. Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell

programme.title: Prince Charles: Inside the Duchy of Cornwall
programme.desc: Observational documentary going behind the scenes of Prince Charles's private estate the Duchy of Cornwall, which he has built up over the past 50 years into a business to support himself, his immediate family and his charities. The first of two parts reveals how the Duchy is run to reflect the prince's personal philosophies, and also explores how the estate supports an ancient way of life on Bodmin Moor but also gives young families the opportunity to embark on a new start in farming

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The master of Gresham College, amateur astronomer Andrew Crompton, is found dead at the Oxford University observatory, and Lewis finds there are numerous suspects, from senior academics to college scouts. Laura Hobson's involvement with a local orchestra helps her give the detective inside information on the complex relationships within the close-knit community, and Hathaway's sharp mind cracks an astronomical conundrum as the team slowly unravels the riddle of the victim's death. Guest starring Sophie Ward (Heartbeat), Diana Quick (Brideshead Revisited) and Robert Hardy (Cornelius Fudge in the Harry Potter films)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The discovery of a body in a shallow grave by the Thames prompts Brooks and Devlin to reopen an eight-year-old murder case, casting doubt on a conviction made by Steel at the time. When the imprisoned man, Luke Slade, is given leave to appeal, he represents himself in court - but what starts as a legal battle soon develops into a personal vendetta. Crime drama, guest starring Iain Glen

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin reopen a 25-year-old case when the bones of a young boy are found buried inside the wall of a communal basement. The victim's former best friend takes part in therapy to revisit her memory of the night he disappeared and reveals a shocking secret, but Phillips and Steel worry her claims will not convince a jury. Guest starring Keith Barron and Colin Salmon, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Freema Agyeman and Ben Daniels

programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Animal Orphans
programme.desc: Comedian Paul O'Grady returns with another Animal Orphans, and having previously visited Africa, he now travels farther east to find out about the rehabilitation of orphaned wild animals in the Malaysian part of Borneo. In the first of two parts, he heads for the orang-utan rehabilitation centre in Sepilok, where he discovers how the animals are being threatened by forest fires, learns about the damage that overfishing is doing to sea turtles' natural habitat, and tries to persuade a gibbon to climb a tree - a skill it needs to learn before it can return to the wild

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Aircraft manufacturer Lord Mayfield hosts a party to trap a woman suspected of being a spy for the Germans. Fearing events might not go according to plan, Lady Mayfield decides to summon the famous Belgian detective to keep an eye on proceedings. Starring David Suchet, with John Stride and Phyllida Law

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Andrea tries to convince Jamie that he must have hit an animal, but Lydia arrives to reveal Moira has been the victim of a hit and run and Jamie struggles to take in the news. Harriet rejects Will's suggestion that they attend a wedding fair. while Amelia is furious when she opens a 'get well soon' card to Dan from Brenda

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jamie worries about keeping the truth from Kim, but Andrea tells him they must convince her that they are back together for good. Moira lays unconscious in hospital, and Cain talks to the nurse about her condition, anxious he may lose her, while Bob is adamant he can run the cafe while Brenda is away

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Andy and Steve urge Liz to change her mind about divorcing Jim. Liz asks Des to give her a job. Joyce is intrigued to discover Vera's son is in prison

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre hopes Mike will give her a job in his factory. Sean grows interested in Liz when he hears she has separated from Jim. Andy challenges his mum over her relationship with Des

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris tells Rachel he wants her back. Kim pressures Dave into going for a drink with her. Vic plays a practical joke on Viv and it explosively backfires

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Viv feels humiliated by Vic, Jack's pessimism frustrates Sarah when they look for a new farm to buy, and Rachel makes a fool of herself when she attempts to seduce Steve

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Ventress thinks Mike's replacement PC Steve Crane is too anxious to prove his worth as he investigates a spate of farm break-ins on the moors, and Vernon causes chaos trying to impress new arrival Dr Liz Merrick with his community spirit. Elsewhere, Mike grows disillusioned with CID work. James Carlton and Aislin McGuckin star as the newcomers

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid has problems when Jean pleads for a new freezer

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean and Sally interpret Sid's dreams and Trevor tries to recreate the atmosphere of the previous night

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Angie persuades Robert to arrange an evening out with the lads _ but neither she nor Ida are prepared for the consequences

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A barman from rural inn the Maid in Splendour is shot dead as he walks near a ruined cottage in woodlands - and Barnaby's investigation reveals a clash between charming landlord Michael Bannerman, the unofficial squire of Midsomer Worthy, and his abrasive son Stephen, who now runs the pub. John Nettles and John Hopkins star, with guest appearances by William Gaunt, Alan Cox, Dave Hill and Frances Tomelty

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An informant reveals two local hoodlums are planning to raid a shipment of gold bullion from Haworth Point, prompting Shiner to set up an ambush to catch the would-be thieves. Gina receives a visit from her niece, who has fallen in with a bad crowd back in Manchester, while Liz enlists David to paint the surgery and sitting room - but is less than pleased with the results. Drama, starring Simon Molloy and Tricia Penrose

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank is embarrassed by his behaviour towards Tina. Biff wants to know why Dave is rushing into marriage. The Dingles hope to clean up selling Christmas stock in the village

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak is not happy in his role as Santa, Kathy and Dave's wedding day arrives, and Vic catches Viv and Terry sharing a romantic moment together

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Josie applies for a job as office manager with Mike. Andy tries to get Steve to see that Liz as much to blame as Jim. Fiona throws a grand opening party for her salon

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim returns from Ireland determined to put things right with Liz, but she refuses to see him. Maureen is annoyed when Reg calls off visiting her at the weekend

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth is called to Gossington Hall by her old friend Dolly Bantry (Joanna Lumley) when she finds the corpse of an unknown blonde in the library of her home. It soon transpires that there is no shortage of suspects, including local bad boy Basil Blake (Ben Miller) and Dolly's husband Arthur (James Fox), but when the victim is identified as a dancer in line to inherit a fortune, enquiries take a different turn. Crime drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Simon Callow, Jack Davenport and Tara Fitzgerald

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An informant reveals two local hoodlums are planning to raid a shipment of gold bullion from Haworth Point, prompting Shiner to set up an ambush to catch the would-be thieves. Gina receives a visit from her niece, who has fallen in with a bad crowd back in Manchester, while Liz enlists David to paint the surgery and sitting room - but is less than pleased with the results. Drama, starring Simon Molloy and Tricia Penrose

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The doctor spends his last day in Portwenn seeing an influx of patients as he prepares to close down the surgery and move to London. As he bids farewell to old friends and sets off to leave the village, he sees Louisa's teaching assistant Tasha fall from the Harbour Day stage and stops to help. But the bigger hold-up comes when pregnant Louisa is involved in a car accident. Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz star

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The curmudgeonly GP struggles to come to terms with his new role as a father, having planned to resume his career as a consultant in London before the arrival of his and Louisa's as-yet unnamed son. To make matters worse, he begins to have serious doubts about the competency of his replacement Dr Di Dibbs when she misdiagnoses a patient as well as herself. Martin is then dealt a devastating personal blow after hearing his aunt Joan has died. Starring Martin Clunes, Joanna Scanlan and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin reopen a 25-year-old case when the bones of a young boy are found buried inside the wall of a communal basement. The victim's former best friend takes part in therapy to revisit her memory of the night he disappeared and reveals a shocking secret, but Phillips and Steel worry her claims will not convince a jury. Guest starring Keith Barron and Colin Salmon, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber, Freema Agyeman and Ben Daniels

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet moves a step closer to tracking down Veronica's killer while investigating a recent murder that bears similarities to the cold case. Rachel finds out about an affair Nick once had with a juror in the middle of a trial - a revelation that could end his career. Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp, Suranne Jones and Rupert Graves

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack learns he is tipped for promotion with the uniform branch and even more flattered to discover he has a secret admirer, but before he has time to bask in this rare moment of glory a child murder claims his attention. Linking the disappearance of a 10-year-old boy to a mysterious hit-and-run killing, he is soon mired in a complex investigation and fuming about a bureaucratic blunder that leaves a known paedophile walking free on his patch. David Jason stars

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is asked to investigate the disappearance of a bank's most valued customer, who had been planning to sell the chairman a map showing where to find a long-lost silver mine. David Suchet stars

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida has a run-in with the law while changing a purchase at the shop

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster teams up with a dashing man to investigate a series of poison-pen letters in the sleepy village of Lymstock. Two of the missives have led to the deaths of Mona Symmington, the wife of Lymstock solicitor Richard, and Agnes Woddell, the parlour maid at the Symmingtons' house. However, Miss Marple suspects there is more to these deaths than meets the eye. Murder mystery with an all-star cast, featuring Geraldine McEwan, James D'Arcy, Emilia Fox, Imogen Stubbs, Ken Russell, Harry Enfield and Kelly Brook

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Yasmeen is stunned by Geoff's latest move, while Imran tentatively broaches the subject of fostering with Nick and explains how they've put their plans on hold so as not to upset Leanne. Kevin breaks the news to Abi that the twins are emigrating to Australia, while Aggie implores Grace to think twice before ending her relationship with Michael

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alya fears that Geoff may win Yasmeen back and Kevin urges Abi to meet up with the twins one last time before they leave for Australia or she will live to regret it. Carla calls in at the factory and admits to Nick that she's bored of pulling pints and feels it's time she got back into the business, while Adam tells Gary it's obvious he's involved in some dodgy deal

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin asks Abi to move in with him and Jack, but, while she is delighted, Seb is angry that she has moved on so quickly from her own kids to Kevin's. Geoff promises Yasmeen that when she comes home, things will be different and he'll seek professional help, while the factory girls are shocked when they realise Carla is once more their boss

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A well-known eccentric contacts the Belgian sleuth when he is haunted by suicidal nightmares. However, he fails to follow Poirot's advice, and is found dead soon afterwards. Drama, starring David Suchet, with Joely Richardson and Mary Tamm

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Grizzled copper Jack Frost teams up with an obnoxious, chain-smoking colleague to investigate a seemingly straightforward hit-and-run incident that proves to have devastating repercussions for the victim's family and their peaceful rural community. David Jason, Bruce Alexander and Philip Jackson star

programme.title: The Great St Trinian's Train Robbery (1966)
programme.desc: A crook and his cronies stash the money from a train heist in a deserted old mansion, planning to recover it in good time. However, several years later, when the thieves arrive to collect their loot, they discover the building has been turned into a school for girls where the pupils have become infamous for their escapades. Comedy, starring Frankie Howerd, Dora Bryan, Reg Varney and George Cole

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster meets fellow enthusiasts Tommy and Tuppence at a nursing home, where the ramblings of an elderly resident prompt them to join forces and investigate the unsolved mystery of a murdered child. The trail leads to the Norfolk village of Farrell St Edmund, a community harbouring sinister secrets and tales of heartbreak. Geraldine McEwan heads an all-star cast, including Greta Scacchi, Anthony Andrews, Charles Dance, June Whitfield and Claire Bloom

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The launch of the latest sparkling wine by Midsomer Vinae Winery falls flat when critic Nadia Simons gives it a scathing review, then guests sampling the vintage start to collapse. Owner William Carnarvon suspects the Farmers' Wives Association of trying to destroy his business, while his wife Diana frets over her mother Matilda, one of the victims. Pathologist Kate reveals the drink was laced with slug poison, but how is the attack connected to the death of a child in a hit-and-run? Barnaby and Nelson are called in to investigate. Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee star in the mystery drama, with Claire Bloom, Mark Bonnar, Rosie Cavaliero and Ruth Gemmell

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A land dispute escalates during a cowboy fair run by the Wild West Society in the village of Ford Florey. Farmers Jack Fincher and Silas Burbage both claim to own a patch of wasteland known as the Swamp, but Dan Malko wants to buy it and create a travellers' site. Barnaby and Jones are called in when the feud leads to murder during the Wild West-style shoot-outs at the annual event. Guest starring Kenneth Cranham, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of Midsomer resident and biscuit factory boss Eric Calder leads Barnaby and Nelson to Copenhagen. The pair join forces with local detectives Birgitte Poulsen and Anna Degn in the hunt for the killer, and during the course of their investigation they discover the businessman had numerous links to the capital. Anglo-Danish collaboration originally shown to mark Midsomer's 100th episode, guest starring Ann Eleonora Jorgensen (The Killing), Birgitte Hjort Sorensen (Borgen), Sanjeev Bhaskar and Joanna Scanlan, alongside Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The evacuation of a central London hospital leads to the discovery of a tragic death. When Brooks and Devlin investigate, a suspect is arrested but the prosecutors (Ben Daniels and Freema Agyeman) face an uphill struggle building a case - so they team up with the detectives in a bid to gather more evidence. Patrick Malahide and Bill Paterson co-star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin fail to find a lead when a 13-year-old is kicked to death at Euston Station, so they turn to Chandler, who holds a press conference. As the detectives are led through a tangled web of family loyalties, the suspect's barrister, an old flame of Steel's, gathers evidence for an audacious defence strategy that could change the nature of the British legal system. Dervla Kirwan guest stars, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A former vice cop is found beaten to death, setting Brooks and Devlin off on an investigation through London's seedy underbelly. The victim's widow and his former boss both seem to know more about the murder than they are letting on - but the trail eventually leads to a respectable children's clothes shop. Crime drama, with guest appearances by Sean Pertwee (Cold Feet), Lesley Manville (Cranford) and Juliet Aubrey (Primeval)

programme.title: Barging Round Britain with John Sergeant
programme.desc: The broadcaster returns to travel more of Britain's canal network, exploring the extraordinary stories of the people and places on the way. The series starts with John navigating the Peak Forest Canal, which runs through stunning countryside from Buxworth in Derbyshire to Ashton-under-Lyne. Along the way, in Hyde he meets former boxer Ricky Hatton, who discusses his plans to revitalise the area and offer new opportunities to local youngsters, and joins a team of junior archaeologists at Mellor Mill, who are attempting to unearth the remains of what was once one of the world's largest mills

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida is adamant she's not available for work when the employment agency gets in touch _ until she takes a call

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack is asked to investigate the theft of a car belonging to a friend of Mullett, but misjudges the grizzled Superintendent's capacity for tact and diplomacy and it is not long before he is taken off the case. Things take a shocking turn when the car finally turns up - with the body of a drug dealer in the boot. Detective drama, starring David Jason, with Melanie Thaw and Bruce Alexander

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A blizzard forces the sleuthing spinster to spend a night in isolated Sittaford House, situated on a Devon moor. Without explanation, the building's owner, up-and-coming politician Clive Trevelyan, decides to spend the evening in a local hotel - only for a Ouija board to predict his murder. Mystery drama with an all-star cast, including Geraldine McEwan, Robert Hardy, Matthew Kelly, Patricia Hodge and Mel Smith

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour detective Jack Frost investigates a woman's near-fatal shooting. The victim was all set to testify against a notorious drugs baron accused of murdering one of his employees, but the appearance of a contract killer makes her think again - and it appears someone on Jack's team is leaking information. Meanwhile, the station is turned upside down in the hunt for a missing lottery ticket. David Jason, Michael McKell and Bruce Alexander star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper detective accepts an invitation to spend yuletide at the house of an elderly millionaire who seems intent on provoking his money-grubbing sons into murdering him. At first he tries hard to ignore the strained atmosphere and relax - but soon finds himself with a gruesome mystery on his hands. Agatha Christie whodunit, starring David Suchet and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: At the Luxton Deeping Crime Festival, the unveiling of a newly discovered novel by deceased Midsomer crime writer George Summersbee is jeopardised when the manuscript is stolen and local artist Suzie Colebrooke is electrocuted by a booby-trapped roulette wheel. Can new father Barnaby untangle a web of jealousy and obsession to find the killer? With Fiona Dolman, Tamzin Malleson, Liberty Mills, Una Stubbs and Georgia Taylor

programme.title: Only When I Laugh
programme.desc: Norman's overbearing mother tries her hand at matchmaking, while Figgis's visitors cause him some anxiety. Comedy, with James Bolam, Christopher Strauli and Peter Bowles

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: A temperamental film actress becomes the apparent victim of an abduction from the set of her latest movie, and Simon must find out whether her disappearance is a publicity stunt or something altogether more sinister. Roger Moore stars

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: The outlaws are attacked by a group of angry Crusaders who believe Robin is a thief. Michael Praed stars

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: In order to investigate a villain's idea of the perfect crime, Harriet poses as a prostitute and Jim goes up in the world. Police drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur's niece is set to be married and he has agreed to supply the wedding limousine - but her big day is placed in jeopardy when he finds he also needs the vehicle to shift a consignment of pornographic magazines. Warren Clarke guest stars, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle considers his future in CI5 after it transpires the father of his fiancee is under surveillance by the unit, suspected of being part of an international drug-smuggling ring. Patricia Hodge guest stars, with Martin Shaw

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan and Carter stake out a dodgy garage, believed to be the base of a criminal gang. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star in the first episode of the crime-busting drama, with Ian Hendry, Brian Blessed and June Brown

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon visits the West Indies, but his excursion turns into an investigation into the murder of an old friend. Sixties drama, starring Roger Moore

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: A lovelorn, smooth-talking minstrel turns to Robin and his outlaw band for help in rescuing his beloved from the Sheriff of Nottingham's clutches. Michael Praed stars

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: The pair are caught between two rival gangs in a world of extortion, murder and kidnapping. Police drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is held by the police after a car he is importing from Europe is impounded by drugs officers, so Terry goes in search of those he suspects are responsible for his boss' predicament - the vehicle's original owners. Light-hearted crime drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 enters a deadly race with the KGB to free a suspected double agent while he is in transit from prison to court, in the hope the action will flush out a mole operating inside the unit. Action adventure, starring Gordon Jackson, Lewis Collins and Martin Shaw

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Members of Regan's own flying squad fall under suspicion when £35,000 in cash goes missing during an operation to protect a security van. Detective drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Tour de France ReCycled: Geraint's Tour
programme.desc: Gearint Thomas introduces action from stage 19 of the 2018 race, a mountainous route that took the riders on a 200.5km journey from Lourdes to Laruns

programme.title: World of Sport

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present racing from Newbury and York. At Newbury, the feature race is the Group 3 Hackwood Stakes over six furlongs, which will feature some sprinters hoping for Group One success later in the year. Chief supporting races include the Aphrodite Stakes - transferred from Newmarket - the Rose Bowl Stakes for juveniles and also some competitive handicaps. The feature betting race of the day is at York, where a big field will go to post for the John Smith's Cup over 10 furlongs - a race won by some super horses in recent years. Analysis from Jason Weaver, reporting from Oli Bell and Matt Chapman and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Brewster's Millions (1985)
programme.desc: A baseball player is stunned to learn he has come into a massive inheritance. Unfortunately, there is one condition - he must somehow find a way to spend $30million in just one month, or he will lose the lot. Meanwhile, two scheming lawyers, who stand to get the money if he fails, are determined to sabotage his efforts. Comedy, starring Richard Pryor, John Candy, Lonette McKee and Jerry Orbach

However, the prisoners see a chance to escape during the match. Second World War adventure, with Michael Caine, Sylvester Stallone, Max von Sydow and Daniel Massey.
